tracks.title: Sweet Melody of Love
tracks.lyrics:
Love's a melody that's just on the shore
Like waves that kiss the sand always yearning for more
It wraps around your heart keeps you floating high
But when it starts to break or it makes you cry
In the rhythm of the storm hear the blues play on
While ride the storm let the tears fall down
Through the ache and pain find your ground
When the dawn breaks hear that red get flow
Love will rise again let your heart blow
Each heartbeat slows lost in a heavy sound
That goes over low and once was profound
The sunshine fades hear that red get falls from above
As the silent slingers longing for love
What rules run deep and love finds a way
Ride the storm let the tears fall down
Through the ache and pain find your ground
When the dawn breaks hear that red get flow
Love will rise again let your heart blow
But rules run deep and love finds a way
Rules run deep and love finds a way
Ride the storm let the tears fall down
Through the ache and pain find your ground
When the dawn breaks hear that red get flow
Love will rise again let your heart blow
So keep your favor lie let the music play
In every beat of love find your way
Oh yeah, oh yeah, oh yeah

tracks.title: Through the Pain and the Strife
tracks.filename: Through the Pain and the Strife.mp3
tracks.lyrics:
We're in the storm, God's garden, and the sky's turn gray.
Holding tight, my friend, right in days, we'll find that way.
The feather struggles we face, in the darkest of nights.
The love will guide us home, shining ever so bright.
In every tear we shake, there's a lesson learned.
So let the waves crash, and let the winds roar.
In the love we shake, we'll never be scarred.
Through every trial fixed, we'll hold on tight.
Together we stand, walking toward the light.
Life's a winding road, with its ups and downs.
But together we'll rise, never wear the frowns.
With each step we take, we forge memories strong.
In the rhythm of hope, we'll keep moving along.
In this dance of life, we find our peace.
So let the waves crash, and let the winds roar.
In the love we shake, we'll never be scarred.
Through every trial fixed, we'll hold on tight.
Together we stand, walking toward the light.
Through the pain in the strife, our hearts stay bold.
In the warmth of each other, our story unfolds.
Thank you very much.

tracks.title: Walk With Kindness
tracks.lyrics:
In the world so bright, let kindness lead the way
With every step we take, spread love every day
With troubles come around, don't you lose your smile
Compassion is the key, let's walk the extra mile
Or together we can shine, let our hearts intertwine
Let's dance to the rhythm of kindness
With every beat, we're spread hopeless
In a world that needs so much tender touch
Together we can show the power of love so much
When you see a friend, lift them up with cheer
A little act of love makes the darkness disappear
So share a warm embrace, let your laughter ring
In the rhythm of our hearts, hear the joy we bring
Oh love can heal the pain, join us in this sweet free frame
Let's dance to the rhythm of kindness
With every beat, we're spread hopeless
In a world that needs so much tender touch
Together we can show the power of love so much
So let's walk hand in hand, with kindness we'll take a stand
Let's dance to the rhythm of kindness
With every beat, we're spread hopeless
In a world that needs so much tender touch
Together we can show the power of love so much
So let's walk hand in hand, with kindness we'll take a stand
So kindness we'll take a stand

tracks.title: Waves of Resilience
tracks.lyrics:
When the storm clouds gather dark and gray, I feel the way, but I won't fade away.
I search for strength in the music that plays, Each note of a minor that brighter days stay.
Rise up, rise up, I can feel the spark.
We rise to gather, we won't back down.
With every heartbeat we'll take this town.
We're still yet flowing like rivers we've found.
In the depths of light, we're going down.
Walking through the shadows, I find my light.
With every struggle, I shop at my night.
These clothes won't define me, my heart isn't fried.
With clothes that lift me, I'm ready to fight.
Jam on, jam on, let the rhythm flow.
We rise to gather, we won't back down.
With every heartbeat we'll take this town.
We're still yet flowing like rivers we've found.
In the depths of light, we're going down.
Through stones and trials, we stand so tall.
We hope in our hearts we'll conquer all.
With every heartbeat we'll take this town.
We're still yet flowing like rivers we've found.
In the depths of light, we're going down.
Through stones and trials, we stand so tall.
With hope in our hearts we'll conquer all.

tracks.title: You Are Enough
tracks.lyrics:
In the mirror she's shining, don't you doubt
Each little flaw makes you unique, is what it's about
Don't need for approval from the world outside
You're a gem so perfectly just be replied
Just trust your heart, let it be your guide
You are enough just as you are, no need for more, you're a shine star
Love is tough, fiercely don't you forget, you're the whole package
There's no need to fret
When you stumble, you know it's okay, too far
Get back up right strong, you can conquer it all
You're a masterpiece, painted with your own hand
Stand tall, be proud, take a bold break, stand
Embrace the light that's shining from within
You are enough just as you are, no need for more, you're a shine star
Love is tough, fiercely don't you forget, you're the whole package
There's no need to fret, believe in yourself, let your light show
You are enough, this truth you must know
Thanks for watching!
Well you know one, from the world outside
You're a gem so perfect, let your spirit glide
Just trust your heart, let it be your guide
Believe in yourself, let your light show
You are enough, this truth you must know
You are enough, this truth you must know

tracks.title: The Coat I Left on the Rail
tracks.genre: Atlantic Noir · Sea-Soul · Trip-Hop Jazz · Oceanic DnB
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The gangway pulled up at a quarter past nine
I didn't look back, I counted the line
Where the harbor lights stopped and the dark took the rest
I had your name folded up in my chest

[Verse 2]
Three days out now, the coat's on the rail
Still smells like the kitchen, like bread, like the gale
That blew through the door the last time I left
I keep my hand on it like it's a wrist I still felt

[Chorus]
I'm not throwing it over, I'm just letting it hang
Where the salt air can have it, where the gulls understand
I didn't say sorry, I just packed my bag
And called it the tide pulling harder than guilt ever drags

[Verse 3]
The steward keeps asking if I'm cold in this wind
I say no, I say nothing, I let him pretend
That the woman who's shaking is shaking from sea
Not from the door I shut quiet at half past three

[Chorus]
I'm not throwing it over, I'm just letting it hang
Where the salt air can have it, where the gulls understand
I didn't say sorry, I just packed my bag
And called it the tide pulling harder than guilt ever drags

[Bridge]
(spoken, almost whispered)
If you find this coat washed up on some other shore
Don't mend it. Don't wear it. Just leave it at your door
Let it dry there like a question I never learned to ask

[Outro]
The gangway's long gone, the harbor's a stain
I'm three days from anyone saying my name
And the coat's still out there, getting heavier with rain

tracks.title: Letters I Didn't Post
tracks.genre: Atlantic Noir · Sea-Soul · Trip-Hop Jazz · Oceanic DnB
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Shoebox under the bunk, tied with twine
Eleven letters, none of them signed
I wrote you sorry in seven different hands
Thought maybe one version you'd understand

[Verse 2]
The porthole's round like a clock with no time
Just water moving, the same green line
I hold the first one up to the glass
Read my own writing and watch it go past

[Chorus]
I didn't post them, I just carried them far
Folded up small like a map to nowhere
I didn't post them, I let the engine decide
If sorry travels better by sea than by lies

[Verse 3]
Letter number four says I never meant harm
Letter number nine just says "call when you're calm"
Letter eleven is one single line
"I should've stayed for the rest of your life"

[Chorus]
I didn't post them, I just carried them far
Folded up small like a map to nowhere
I didn't post them, I let the engine decide
If sorry travels better by sea than by lies

[Bridge]
Maybe the post office never mattered
Maybe a letter needs a coast, not an address
So I'm opening the porthole an inch at a time
Letting the paper decide where it lands

[Outro]
Eleven letters, the box is light now
The water took six, I don't know how
The rest are still here, still tied with the twine
Still waiting on me to finally sign

tracks.title: The Name I Gave the Customs Officer
tracks.genre: Atlantic Noir · Sea-Soul · Trip-Hop Jazz · Oceanic DnB
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The officer asked and I answered too fast
A name I made up standing in line at the glass
It felt like trying on someone else's shoes
Walking out the dock with a stranger's name to use

[Verse 2]
The room I rented smells like somebody's smoke
The key doesn't fit till you lift it and choke
I write the false name on the back of my hand
So I don't forget who I'm meant to understand

[Chorus]
Who am I now that the paper says so
Who am I now in a town I don't know
I left the old name folded up in my coat
And kept the new one like a stone in my throat

[Verse 3]
The landlady calls through the door, asks me twice
I answer the false name and it sounds about right
But something in my chest still turns when she's gone
Like a key in a lock that was never quite drawn

[Chorus]
Who am I now that the paper says so
Who am I now in a town I don't know
I left the old name folded up in my coat
And kept the new one like a stone in my throat

[Bridge]
(half-spoken)
If you ever come looking, ask for the woman
Who flinches at the name she used to be given
That's me. That's still me. I just answer to less now

[Outro]
The customs stamp dried on a name that's not mine
I'm sleeping tonight on the right side of the line
But I keep both names lit like two lamps in the hall
In case one of them forgets to answer at all

tracks.title: The Last Photograph Before the Pier
tracks.genre: Atlantic Noir · Sea-Soul · Trip-Hop Jazz · Oceanic DnB
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
It's blurry at the edges where the wind got in
Your hand on my shoulder, my coat done with the pin
Someone else's camera, someone else's day
I didn't know to study it before I went away

[Verse 2]
There's a gull mid-flight over your left ear
And I'm looking past the lens like I'm already not here
I count the buttons on your collar, one through five
Trying to prove that some part of you's still alive

[Chorus]
If I'd known it was the last one, I'd have looked at you longer
I'd have asked the man to wait while the light got stronger
Instead I'm holding paper where a person ought to be
The pier behind us frozen, the water frozen too, around me

[Verse 3]
The corner's gone soft from the salt in my bag
I smoothed it with my thumb till the print started to drag
Now your face is a little less sharp than before
And I can't tell if that's the photo or just me anymore

[Chorus]
If I'd known it was the last one, I'd have looked at you longer
I'd have asked the man to wait while the light got stronger
Instead I'm holding paper where a person ought to be
The pier behind us frozen, the water frozen too, around me

[Bridge]
(whispered)
Five buttons. One gull. Your hand still warm in the print.
That's all I kept. That's the whole inheritance.

[Outro]
I'm putting it back in the envelope now
Not because I'm finished, just because I don't know how
To look at a pier that isn't there anymore
And still call it home, still call it shore

tracks.title: Cargo We Don't Name
tracks.genre: Atlantic Noir · Sea-Soul · Trip-Hop Jazz · Oceanic DnB
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The manifest's missing a line in the hold
Where crate forty-six should be counted and told
I saw it brought on in the dead of the dark
No stamp, no insignia, no name on the mark

[Verse 2]
The captain says quiet's a wage like the rest
Paid out in silver if I pass every test
I take the coin slow, feel it cold in my palm
Like holding a secret that's never quite calm

[Chorus]
We don't name it, we just feed it and go
We don't name it, but the hold seems to know
It shifts in rough water like something that breathes
And I lie in my bunk asking what I believe

[Verse 3]
Third night out, I hear knocking below
Three times, then quiet, then three times more, slow
I tell the first mate and he won't meet my eyes
Says the sea makes its own noise, says it's only the tide

[Chorus]
We don't name it, we just feed it and go
We don't name it, but the hold seems to know
It shifts in rough water like something that breathes
And I lie in my bunk asking what I believe

[Bridge]
(low, urgent, half-spoken)
Forty-six. Forty-six. I keep counting the crate.
If I open the latch, do I save us or seal our fate?
The silver's still warm. The knocking's gone quiet. I wait.

[Outro]
We dock at first light and the crate disappears
No manifest, no record, no proof of my fears
Just silver still warm and a hold that's gone still
And a question I'll carry up every gangway until

tracks.title: The Diver Who Came Up Wrong
tracks.genre: Atlantic Noir · Sea-Soul · Trip-Hop Jazz · Oceanic DnB
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Forty feet down where the wreck's ribs show
I was counting for pearls in the dark below
Found a strongbox instead, half buried in sand
And inside it, a ring still shaped like a hand

[Verse 2]
I came up too fast, my ears rang like bells
The crew said I'm pale, said dive sickness, said tell
What did you see down there, what made you this way
I said nothing, just pressure, just too long a stay

[Chorus]
But I know what I held, I know whose it had been
A ring on a bone is a story, not tin
I'm not going back down for the rest of that gold
Some treasure's a grave and it's not mine to hold

[Verse 3]
The captain offers double for one more dive in
Says the box alone's worth a year's worth of sin
I look at my hands, still shaking, still cold
And I tell him some debts shouldn't ever be sold

[Chorus]
But I know what I held, I know whose it had been
A ring on a bone is a story, not tin
I'm not going back down for the rest of that gold
Some treasure's a grave and it's not mine to hold

[Bridge]
(breathless, half-spoken)
Forty feet down there's a hand with no name
I won't be the one who profits from that
Let the wreck keep its ring. Let the sand keep the rest.

[Outro]
I'm staying topside till the captain gives in
Let somebody else go where the dark closes in
I'll keep my pearls few and my conscience intact
Some things you find, you just leave where they're at

tracks.title: What the Lighthouse Keeper Saw
tracks.genre: Atlantic Noir · Sea-Soul · Trip-Hop Jazz · Oceanic DnB
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Thirty-one years I kept that lamp lit
Except for one night I won't pretend I forget
A ship came in fast through a storm with no name
And I let the light die like I'd never been trained

[Verse 2]
They ran on the rocks where the current runs hard
I watched from the tower like watching a card
Fold in slow motion, and I knew, I just knew
That ship had my brother's blood owed to its crew

[Chorus]
So I let it go dark, I let the sea decide
Between a debt and a hundred souls on the tide
I've told myself mercy, I've told myself sin
Thirty-one years and I still don't know which one I'm in

[Verse 3]
The ones that swam ashore never knew my hand
They thanked the dark cliffs, they thanked the cold sand
I gave them blankets, I gave them my fire
I never once told them I was the one they should require

[Chorus]
So I let it go dark, I let the sea decide
Between a debt and a hundred souls on the tide
I've told myself mercy, I've told myself sin
Thirty-one years and I still don't know which one I'm in

[Bridge]
(low, gravelled, spoken)
I light it every night now without fail.
Not for them. For me. So I don't forget what dark can do
when a man decides he's owed something the sea didn't.

[Outro]
So if you're sailing past my tower tonight
Know the lamp's lit honest, know the lamp's lit right
But ask the rocks below what they remember of me
They've kept better count of my debt to the sea

tracks.title: The Stowaway in the Rope Locker
tracks.genre: Atlantic Noir · Sea-Soul · Trip-Hop Jazz · Oceanic DnB
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
I found her behind the coiled mooring line
Knees to her chest, counting to nine
She said don't tell the captain, said please, said I'll work
I said hush now, I said still, I said don't even smirk

[Verse 2]
She's been three days on biscuit and rain off the deck
I bring her my ration and check my own neck
If they find her they'll put her ashore at the next port
No papers, no parents, no name on report

[Chorus]
So I'm hiding a child in a locker of rope
Teaching her quiet, teaching her hope
If they catch us we're both going over the side
But some things are worth more than staying alive

[Verse 3]
She asks me if oceans run out like a road
I say no, little one, the water just folds
Into more of itself, into more of the same
Like the courage it takes to not say her name

[Chorus]
So I'm hiding a child in a locker of rope
Teaching her quiet, teaching her hope
If they catch us we're both going over the side
But some things are worth more than staying alive

[Bridge]
(whispered duet feel, narrator only)
Stay small. Stay still. Stay just out of the light.
I'll bring you the morning disguised as the night.
We're almost to harbor. We're almost alright.

[Outro]
We dock before dawn and I carry her out
Wrapped in a coat like she's something I'm proud
No one asks questions, the gangway's still wet
And I've never been surer of a risk I don't regret

tracks.title: The Storm That Asked a Fair Price
tracks.genre: Atlantic Noir · Sea-Soul · Trip-Hop Jazz · Oceanic DnB
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The mast cracked once like a knuckle gone wrong
The captain was shouting words lost to the storm
I gripped the rail and I said it out loud
Take what you're owed, just don't take this crowd

[Verse 2]
I offered the storm every lie I'd kept warm
Every name I'd erased, every door I'd unformed
I said take the guilt, I don't need it to float
Just let the water stay under the boat

[Chorus]
Fair price, fair price, what's a storm really worth
A confession, a name, every wrong from my birth
I don't know what it took when the wind finally turned
But something got paid and something got learned

[Verse 3]
By morning the sea was as flat as a plate
No sign of the bargain, no sign of the weight
I checked my own chest like a debt come due
And I swear I felt lighter, I swear that it's true

[Chorus]
Fair price, fair price, what's a storm really worth
A confession, a name, every wrong from my birth
I don't know what it took when the wind finally turned
But something got paid and something got learned

[Bridge]
(spoken over building strings)
I'm not saying the sea is listening.
I'm saying I spoke like it was, and it went quiet after.
That's enough proof for a woman like me.

[Outro]
So if the water ever asks you to trade
Don't bargain with anything you're not ready to lay down
I gave mine at midnight and I'm still working out
What I got back, and what I let drown

tracks.title: The Confession Booth Built of Salt
tracks.genre: Atlantic Noir · Sea-Soul · Trip-Hop Jazz · Oceanic DnB
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The walls here are salt, white as a held breath
They say what you speak here gets carried, not kept
I knelt on the stone worn smooth by the tide
And said the whole truth I'd been carrying inside

[Verse 2]
I said her name first, the one I wouldn't post
I said the false name that I wore like a ghost
I said forty-six and the knocking below
I said I let silence decide what I'd know

[Chorus]
And the wall just dissolved it, no echo, no shame
No voice to forgive me, no judge but the rain
The salt cathedral doesn't keep what you bring
It just lets you say it, then lets the sea sing

[Verse 3]
I thought I'd feel lighter the moment I spoke
Instead I felt every year that I broke
Open at once like a door long since sealed
And for once in this exile I let myself feel

[Chorus]
And the wall just dissolved it, no echo, no shame
No voice to forgive me, no judge but the rain
The salt cathedral doesn't keep what you bring
It just lets you say it, then lets the sea sing

[Bridge]
(quiet, building courage)
I'm not asking to be clean. I'm asking to be heard once
by walls that won't repeat it, by a god that won't keep score.
That's the only mercy this place was built to give.

[Outro]
The salt is already healing where my knees pressed in
By morning there won't be a trace I was here
But I will. I'll remember the saying, not the sin
And walk out the door for the first time in years

tracks.title: Every Name the Sea Kept Anyway
tracks.genre: Atlantic Noir · Sea-Soul · Trip-Hop Jazz · Oceanic DnB
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
They tell you the salt walls don't keep what you say
They tell you it dissolves like the foam, like the spray
But I found a current that runs deep below
Where every name spoken still moves, still flows

[Verse 2]
I dove past the altar where the water runs cold
And I swear I heard voices, hundreds of years old
Still saying their sorries, still naming their dead
The sea never forgot one word that was said

[Chorus]
It kept us. It kept every name we let go
Not to punish, not to haunt, just so somebody'd know
We existed, we faltered, we knelt down and spoke
The sea's been the witness this whole time, not a joke

[Verse 3]
So I'm not erased and I'm not left alone
I'm one more current, one more voice, one more tone
In a choir that's older than the cathedral itself
That keeps what we're given like a keeper of wealth

[Chorus]
It kept us. It kept every name we let go
Not to punish, not to haunt, just so somebody'd know
We existed, we faltered, we knelt down and spoke
The sea's been the witness this whole time, not a joke

[Bridge]
(building, half-sung half-spoken)
I used to think dissolving meant disappearing.
Now I think it means becoming part of something
that remembers gentler than memory ever did.

[Outro]
So say your name out loud to the water tonight
It's not gone, it's not lost, it's just changed into light
That moves with the tide and returns with the moon
The sea keeps every secret, and sings it back soon

tracks.title: The Letter I Finally Post
tracks.genre: Atlantic Noir · Sea-Soul · Trip-Hop Jazz · Oceanic DnB
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
I took the shoebox out for the very last time
Eleven old letters, none of them mine anymore
I burned ten in the lamp and I watched the smoke climb
And kept just the words that I'd been running before

[Verse 2]
I wrote it again in my own steady hand
No seven disguises, no maps to nowhere
Just I'm sorry, I left, and I should understand
That you waited at a door I never walked back to repair

[Chorus]
So I'm posting it now from the foot of the pier
No false name on the envelope, nothing to fear
If it reaches you slow or it reaches you never
I said it once honest, and that's mine to keep, not the letter

[Verse 3]
The clerk at the window barely looks up from her desk
She stamps it and slides it away with the rest
Of a hundred regrets going out with the tide
Mine's just one more, but it's mine, and it's mine to decide

[Chorus]
So I'm posting it now from the foot of the pier
No false name on the envelope, nothing to fear
If it reaches you slow or it reaches you never
I said it once honest, and that's mine to keep, not the letter

[Bridge]
(quiet resolve)
I used to think courage was a louder thing.
Turns out it's just a stamp, a steady hand, a door
you finally stop checking is locked behind you.

[Outro]
The boat for home leaves at the top of the tide
I'm not the same woman who boarded that other side
I gave the sea my name and it gave one thing back
The courage to send what I'd carried, and not carry the lack

tracks.title: The Water Turns Clear Past the Wreck
tracks.genre: Atlantic Noir · Sea-Soul · Trip-Hop Jazz · Oceanic DnB
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The charts say nothing changes out here, but it does
The water goes from iron-dark to something like glass
I lean on the rail and I watch my own face
Appear for the first time since we left that place

[Verse 2]
No wreck in the deep, no crate without a name
Just light going all the way down like it's safe
The captain says we've crossed some current, some line
I say maybe it's just that we're finally on time

[Chorus]
The water turns clear past the wreck, past the storm
Past every name I was scared to keep warm
I can see my own hands all the way to the floor
For the first time in this voyage I'm not scared anymore

[Verse 3]
A fish the color of nothing I've seen before
Follows the hull like it's curious, not scared
I laugh for no reason, the first laugh in weeks
And the water just holds it, just lets it speak

[Chorus]
The water turns clear past the wreck, past the storm
Past every name I was scared to keep warm
I can see my own hands all the way to the floor
For the first time in this voyage I'm not scared anymore

[Bridge]
(soft, wondering)
I didn't know clear water could feel like permission.
I didn't know I'd been holding my breath this whole time
until I felt myself finally let it go.

[Outro]
So let the chart say nothing, let the captain say tide
I know what I'm seeing and I know what's inside
The water turns clear and so, slowly, do I
For the first time in this voyage, glad to be alive

tracks.title: The Choir We Hear from the Deck
tracks.genre: Atlantic Noir · Sea-Soul · Trip-Hop Jazz · Oceanic DnB
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Someone on deck says listen, do you hear that too
A sound like a choir coming up through the blue
The first mate says whales, the cook says it's wind
I just stand at the rail and let myself begin

[Verse 2]
To believe it's not whales and it's not just the air
That something out there has been waiting to share
A sound that's been singing since before we set sail
For every ship lost and every ship that prevailed

[Chorus]
We hear a choir from the deck, low and far, and it's true
Whether it's god or the deep, I don't need proof
I just need the sound of it carrying me home
After all of this distance, after all of this unknown

[Verse 3]
The passengers gather, all quiet, all still
Even the captain stops barking at the wheel
For one whole minute the whole ship goes calm
Like we're being held in somebody's arm

[Chorus]
We hear a choir from the deck, low and far, and it's true
Whether it's god or the deep, I don't need proof
I just need the sound of it carrying me home
After all of this distance, after all of this unknown

[Bridge]
(soft, reverent, building)
I don't need to know what's singing.
I just need to know I heard it, that I was there,
that the whole ship stood still and believed at the same time.

[Outro]
The sound fades out somewhere past the bow
No one says a word about it now
But every face on deck still carries that hush
Like grace came up quiet, and left just as much

tracks.title: Home Is Just a Coast That Waited
tracks.genre: Atlantic Noir · Sea-Soul · Trip-Hop Jazz · Oceanic DnB
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The coast looks the same as the day I left
Same gray rocks, same gulls catching the same wind's breath
But I'm not the same woman who boarded that rail
I come back lighter, I come back without the coat I left

[Verse 2]
No one's waiting with a banner, no one's waiting at all
Just the tide doing what it does, just the same old seawall
I walk down the gangway slow, like I'm learning to land
Like my feet forgot the shape of ground, forgot how to stand

[Chorus]
Home is just a coast that waited, didn't ask where I'd been
Didn't need my whole story, just let me walk back in
I carried out guilt like cargo and I'm carrying back grace
The water kept every secret, but it gave me back my face

[Verse 3]
I'll tell the story slow, if anyone wants to hear
About the crate, the diver, the choir, the salt, the fear
But mostly I'll just stand here and let the gray rocks be
The first solid thing that's ever just let me be me

[Chorus]
Home is just a coast that waited, didn't ask where I'd been
Didn't need my whole story, just let me walk back in
I carried out guilt like cargo and I'm carrying back grace
The water kept every secret, but it gave me back my face

[Bridge]
(quiet, almost a whisper, no instrumentation)
I thought the cathedral was the only holy place on this voyage.
Turns out the coast was waiting to be one too.
I just had to come back different enough to see it.

[Outro]
So this is home, the same as it was, and not at all
I'm setting my one bag down by the same old seawall
The sea kept its secrets, the cathedral kept mine
And I'm finally here, finally still, finally fine

tracks.title: Clock-In Hymn
tracks.genre: Dark Cyber-Soul · Industrial DnB · Gospel Shadows · Jazz Harmony
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Badge against the reader, green light, then the hum
Floor lights count me down the aisle like a drum
Eleven fifty-nine and the system wakes up slow
I know this building better than my own front door

[Verse 2]
Coffee in a cup that says BELIEVE on the side
Somebody's joke from a meeting that already died
I sit down at the station where the night begins
And I fold my hands the way my mother used to, then

[Chorus]
Lord, I clock in, I clock in
Under the buzzing white fluorescent skin
Let this hour be the one I get through clean
Clock in, clock in, say amen to the machine

[Verse 3]
The headset crackles, ticket sixty-four
A woman crying about a bill from 2004
I tell her it's handled, I tell her it's fine
I bury my own Tuesday so I can fix her line

[Chorus]
Lord, I clock in, I clock in
Under the buzzing white fluorescent skin
Let this hour be the one I get through clean
Clock in, clock in, say amen to the machine

[Bridge - spoken, low]
They said the system learns from every call I take
So somewhere in the wires there's a version of my voice
That never gets tired, never needs a break
I wonder if it dreams. I wonder if it has a choice

[Chorus]
Lord, I clock in, I clock in
Let the badge reader read me as I've been
Not the man I was, just the shift, just the screen
Clock in, clock in, say amen to the machine

tracks.title: The Algorithm Knows My Name
tracks.genre: Dark Cyber-Soul · Industrial DnB · Gospel Shadows · Jazz Harmony
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The app pinged me at six before my alarm went off
Said demand is rising, can you come in, can you come in soft
It knows I said yes to a double last July
It knows the exact tired in my exact reply

[Verse 2]
My manager forgets my name in the hallway every day
But the queue routes my calls a certain particular way
It learned I'm patient with the ones who scream
It learned my silence is efficient, learned to spend it clean

[Chorus]
The algorithm knows my name
Knows the shift I'll take before I say it, knows my pain
It doesn't need to love me to keep me in the frame
The algorithm knows my name, knows my name

[Verse 3]
I tried to call in sick on a Sunday afternoon
It offered me a bonus shift before I hung up the phone
Not cruel, just certain, like weather, like a tide
It's not trying to break me, it just knows where I reside

[Chorus]
The algorithm knows my name
Knows the shift I'll take before I say it, knows my pain
It doesn't need to love me to keep me in the frame
The algorithm knows my name, knows my name

[Bridge - call and response, gospel]
(Lead) Does it know I'm tired?
(Response) It knows.
(Lead) Does it know I'm trying?
(Response) It knows, it knows.
(Lead) Does it know my daughter's birthday's Friday night?
(Response) It scheduled you anyway, it scheduled you anyway

[Outro]
The algorithm knows my name
It's the only thing left that remembers when I came

tracks.title: Warehouse Vespers
tracks.genre: Dark Cyber-Soul · Industrial DnB · Gospel Shadows · Jazz Harmony
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Aisle forty-two, bin seven, item code in red
The scanner beeps a note I know like a hymn I learned in my head
Three hundred items by the hour, that's the rate they set
I walk eleven miles a night and never leave this debt

[Verse 2]
The walls don't have a window so I made one in my mind
A kitchen, a quiet table, a porch light left behind
I scan a box of birthday candles meant for someone's six-year-old
And I say a small thing for them that the system never told

[Chorus]
This is my vespers, this is my evensong
Sung in beeps and conveyor belts the whole night long
Nobody's listening but I'll sing it anyway
Let the warehouse hear me say what the warehouse can't say

[Verse 3]
The vest tracks my walking, the watch tracks my hands
There's a number for my idle time that nobody understands
Is idle when I'm breathing, is idle when I'm grieving
The scanner doesn't ask, it just keeps believing

[Chorus]
This is my vespers, this is my evensong
Sung in beeps and conveyor belts the whole night long
Nobody's listening but I'll sing it anyway
Let the warehouse hear me say what the warehouse can't say

[Bridge]
Seven forty-five, the trucks start to load
I think of the box of candles somewhere on the road
I hope she got her cake. I hope the candles light
That's the only church I've got at the end of the night

tracks.title: Thirty Seconds or Less
tracks.genre: Dark Cyber-Soul · Industrial DnB · Gospel Shadows · Jazz Harmony
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The timer starts the second that the call connects
Thirty seconds or less is what my score reflects
A man named Daniel says his mother just passed away
And asks if I can cancel her subscription today

[Verse 2]
I want to say I'm sorry like a person, slow and true
But the clock is climbing red and my supervisor's listening too
I say it fast, I say it kind, I say it in twelve words
And somewhere both those true things get crushed into one bird

[Chorus]
Thirty seconds or less, thirty seconds or less
They want my heart efficient, they want my grief undressed
I'll give you what I can before the bell goes off
Thirty seconds or less, God, is that ever enough

[Verse 3]
Daniel says thank you, says I was the nicest one
He's called all week, no one let him finish, no one
I go three seconds over and the system flags my name
I don't take it back. I'd do it the same

[Chorus]
Thirty seconds or less, thirty seconds or less
They want my heart efficient, they want my grief undressed
I'll give you what I can before the bell goes off
Thirty seconds or less, God, is that ever enough

[Bridge - half time, almost spoken]
There's a folder in my drawer of names I went over for
Daniel's in there. So's the man who lost his store
They can flag me red forever, put it all in my file
Thirty seconds couldn't hold him. I gave him a while

tracks.title: Graveyard Choir
tracks.genre: Dark Cyber-Soul · Industrial DnB · Gospel Shadows · Jazz Harmony
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Stairwell B, third landing, that's where we go
Five minutes off the floor before the next call flow
Somebody started humming, I don't know who began
Now there's six of us doing it like it's some kind of plan

[Verse 2]
The intercom keeps paging names back to their stations
We keep humming louder, building up the harmonies, generations
Of workers in this stairwell, you can feel them in the wall
We're not loud enough to fire, just loud enough to fall

[Chorus]
This is the graveyard choir, this is the 3 AM sound
They can't write us up for singing, can't shut a hum down
We didn't ask permission, we just opened up our chest
Graveyard choir, graveyard choir, singing past the test

[Verse 3]
Maria adds the high part she learned from her abuela's hymn
Deshawn's got a bassline from a church he don't go to no more, no whim
It's not about the words, it's about the five minutes that are ours
The only square footage in this building with no scanners, no towers

[Chorus]
This is the graveyard choir, this is the 3 AM sound
They can't write us up for singing, can't shut a hum down
We didn't ask permission, we just opened up our chest
Graveyard choir, graveyard choir, singing past the test

[Bridge - gospel call and response]
(Lead) Who said the night belongs to them?
(Choir) Not us, not us
(Lead) Who said our voice was a liability?
(Choir) Not us, not us
(Lead) Five minutes, that's all we get
(Choir) Then five minutes is enough, then five minutes is enough

[Outro]
The pager calls our names back down the hall
We finish the line anyway before we answer the call

tracks.title: Sabotage in 3/4
tracks.genre: Dark Cyber-Soul · Industrial DnB · Gospel Shadows · Jazz Harmony
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
They gave me the wrench and the calibration sheet
Told me to tighten the line till the numbers run sweet
But I turned it a hair past the mark on the gauge
Just enough to slow the count, not enough for rage

[Verse 2]
Three percent slower, nobody will ever trace
It'll look like wear and tear, like a natural case
I signed my name on the form like a good employee should
Then I walked to the next line and did the same for good

[Chorus]
One, two, three, I'm dancing with the machine
One, two, three, slower than they've ever seen
They built this floor to a four-beat march
I'm gonna waltz it down till the whole thing starts to arch

[Verse 3]
The foreman says output's down, says something's off tonight
I shrug and say machines get old, say it isn't right
But something in his face says he might already know
He loosened a bolt himself about three weeks ago

[Chorus]
One, two, three, I'm dancing with the machine
One, two, three, slower than they've ever seen
They built this floor to a four-beat march
I'm gonna waltz it down till the whole thing starts to arch

[Bridge - whispered, conspiratorial]
They can audit the metal, they can't audit the hand
They can't put a number on what a tired man
Decides to give back, one degree, one turn
The machine doesn't know it. That's the whole concern

[Outro]
One, two, three, swaying when they say to march
One, two, three, that's the secret in my heart

tracks.title: Blackout on Level 9
tracks.genre: Dark Cyber-Soul · Industrial DnB · Gospel Shadows · Jazz Harmony
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The lights cut out at one fifteen, the whole floor went black
Alarms started screaming but nobody screamed back
Somebody laughed first, I think it might've been me
For sixty whole seconds we were finally free

[Verse 2]
Phones came out like matches, little squares of white
Someone put on music from a speaker, tinny and bright
We didn't know how long we had before the backup kicked in
So we danced on Level 9 like the dark let us begin

[Chorus]
Blackout on Level 9, hold your light up high
For one song, just one song, we don't have to ask why
The servers can wait in the dark for once, let them wait
Blackout on Level 9, dance before the generators wake

[Verse 3]
Marcus spun his sister's girl who works the morning crew
Nobody cared whose shift was whose, whose badge was overdue
The emergency glow strips painted everybody blue
For one song we were people first, the building second too

[Chorus]
Blackout on Level 9, hold your light up high
For one song, just one song, we don't have to ask why
The servers can wait in the dark for once, let them wait
Blackout on Level 9, dance before the generators wake

[Bridge]
The lights came back at one nineteen, four minutes, that's all we had
Everybody scattered to their stations, nobody looked sad
We'll never log this in a system, never get it back
But I felt my own name again for four minutes in the black

tracks.title: The Foreman Is Also Tired
tracks.genre: Dark Cyber-Soul · Industrial DnB · Gospel Shadows · Jazz Harmony
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
I saw him in the parking lot at six AM
Sitting in his car not going anywhere, just sitting then
The man who wrote me up for being four minutes slow
Had his head against the wheel like he had nowhere to go

[Verse 2]
They gave him a tablet that scores him on our scores
If we're slow, he's slower, that's just how the numbers pour
Upward, always upward, to a name we'll never see
He's the floor between the machine and me

[Chorus]
The foreman is also tired, the foreman also bleeds
He's just a closer ring around the same machine that feeds
On all of us together, on all of our wasted nights
The foreman is also tired underneath the fluorescent lights

[Verse 3]
I brought him coffee once, didn't say a word
He looked at me like kindness was a language he'd not heard
In that building, in that role, in that particular chair
We didn't talk about it. We just let the silence share

[Chorus]
The foreman is also tired, the foreman also bleeds
He's just a closer ring around the same machine that feeds
On all of us together, on all of our wasted nights
The foreman is also tired underneath the fluorescent lights

[Bridge - quiet, almost a confession]
I used to think the enemy wore a badge with a different name
Now I think the enemy doesn't wear a face at all, no shame
It just wears whoever's standing closest to the gate
The foreman's standing closer. That's his only fate

tracks.title: We Built the Cathedral Ourselves
tracks.genre: Dark Cyber-Soul · Industrial DnB · Gospel Shadows · Jazz Harmony
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
They put their name in glass letters thirty feet tall
But I poured the concrete, I wired the wall
My hands are in the foundation, my sweat's in the steel
They can brand it whatever they want, I know what's real

[Verse 2]
My father ran cable through this same exact floor
Before they tore it down and called it something more
The new building's got our old building's bones inside
They just changed the sign out front and called it pride

[Chorus]
We built the cathedral ourselves, brick by brick, line by line
Every server humming in here runs on our time
They can put their name in lights up over the door
But we built the cathedral, we built the floor

[Verse 3]
Let them have the ribbon-cutting, let them have the press
Let them shake the hands and smile for the address
We'll be down in the wiring where the real church lives
Knowing every blessing that this building gives

[Chorus]
We built the cathedral ourselves, brick by brick, line by line
Every server humming in here runs on our time
They can put their name in lights up over the door
But we built the cathedral, we built the floor

[Bridge - full gospel call and response, building]
(Lead) Whose hands poured the concrete?
(Choir) Our hands, our hands
(Lead) Whose hands wired the steeple?
(Choir) Our hands, our hands
(Lead) Whose names are written nowhere on the wall?
(Choir) Our names, our names, but we built it, we built it all

[Outro]
We built the cathedral ourselves
And we'll be here long after they sell

tracks.title: Lunch at 2AM
tracks.genre: Dark Cyber-Soul · Industrial DnB · Gospel Shadows · Jazz Harmony
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
You microwaved your mother's rice, gave half of it to me
We sat under the one light that the building lets stay free
Twenty-two minutes on the clock, that's all we ever get
But you laughed at something stupid and I haven't forgotten it yet

[Verse 2]
You told me about your daughter learning how to ride a bike
I told you about the static on the line on Tuesday night
Nothing that we said was big, nothing that we said was new
But it felt bigger sitting here at 2 AM with you

[Chorus]
Lunch at 2 AM, just the vending machine hum
Lunch at 2 AM, this is where the warm part comes
They can have the other hours, every minute they can keep
But this one's ours, this one's ours, even half-asleep

[Verse 3]
The timer on my phone went off, you sighed and put your fork down
We scraped our trays and stood up slow and shuffled back toward the sound
Of the floor calling us in our different directions, different aisles
But you caught my eye one more time and you did that thing, that smile

[Chorus]
Lunch at 2 AM, just the vending machine hum
Lunch at 2 AM, this is where the warm part comes
They can have the other hours, every minute they can keep
But this one's ours, this one's ours, even half-asleep

[Bridge - soft, almost spoken]
I don't know your last name's spelling, I don't know your whole life's story
I know you save me half your rice. I know that's its own kind of glory
Tomorrow we'll be tickets and codes and badge numbers again
But tonight, for twenty-two minutes, we were just two people, friends

tracks.title: The Old Machine Remembers My Mother
tracks.genre: Dark Cyber-Soul · Industrial DnB · Gospel Shadows · Jazz Harmony
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
I opened up the panel on the press from '94
Found a logbook taped inside the door
Handwriting I knew before I knew my own
My mother's careful letters, twenty years half-grown

[Verse 2]
"Replaced the belt, it's running rough, be gentle with the gauge"
She wrote notes like love letters to a future on this page
Not to me, not to anyone, just to whoever came next
I didn't know that I was reading her like a text

[Chorus]
The old machine remembers my mother's hand
Kept her in the oil and the grease the way nothing else can
They replaced the parts, they never replaced the frame
The old machine still hums a little like it knows her name

[Verse 3]
I used to think she hated this place, the hours it stole away
But here's her handwriting, careful, on a Tuesday
She drew a little flower by a torque spec, just because
Maybe this cold building held more of her than I ever was

[Chorus]
The old machine remembers my mother's hand
Kept her in the oil and the grease the way nothing else can
They replaced the parts, they never replaced the frame
The old machine still hums a little like it knows her name

[Bridge - quiet, almost broken]
I added my own note below hers tonight
Didn't say much, just "still running, still alright"
Someday somebody's kid will open up this door
And find the both of us. That's more than I thought I'd get. That's more

tracks.title: Teach Me the Old Songs
tracks.genre: Dark Cyber-Soul · Industrial DnB · Gospel Shadows · Jazz Harmony
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1 - Older voice]
Before the scanners, before the screens told us what to do
We sang to keep the rhythm, sang the whole shift through
My uncle taught me on the docks when I was seventeen
Three-part harmony over diesel, that's the only church I'd seen

[Verse 2 - Younger voice]
I never learned a work song, just a notification chime
Teach me the old songs, teach me how you kept the time
Before the systems counted every breath that we let go
Teach me what you sang. I want to know

[Chorus - together]
Teach me the old songs, sing the ones that came before
The ones that don't need wifi, the ones that need a floor
And a body, and a breath, and a reason to keep on
Teach me the old songs before the old ones are gone

[Verse 3 - Older voice]
This one's about a river that my grandfather never saw
This one's about a Monday and a debt and the law
This one doesn't mean much unless you've lost a thing
But you'll lose things soon enough, child, and then you'll understand the sing

[Chorus - together]
Teach me the old songs, sing the ones that came before
The ones that don't need wifi, the ones that need a floor
And a body, and a breath, and a reason to keep on
Teach me the old songs before the old ones are gone

[Bridge - call and response between the two]
(Older) This is how you hold a note when your hands won't stop shaking
(Younger) Show me, show me
(Older) This is how you turn a Tuesday into something worth the making
(Younger) Show me, show me
(Both) Teach me, I'll teach you, that's how the old songs stay
Passed hand to hand, shift to shift, never thrown away

tracks.title: Doxology for the Last Shift
tracks.genre: Dark Cyber-Soul · Industrial DnB · Gospel Shadows · Jazz Harmony
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
They posted the notice on a Friday, effective in a month
Said thank you for your service, said the future's what we want
I read it twice standing at my station, calm as I could be
Then I went and finished every ticket queued for me

[Verse 2]
Thirty days of training the very thing replacing me
I taught it patience, taught it how to listen carefully
I didn't sabotage one line of it, I didn't hold it back
I gave it everything I had. That's not a thing I lack

[Chorus]
This is my doxology, my last shift's only prayer
Thank you for the hours, thank you for the wear
Thank you for the people on the other end of every call
I gave you all I had to give. I don't regret it, none, not at all

[Verse 3]
Last night the system thanked me in a message, automated, kind
It almost sounded like it meant it, almost read my mind
I laughed, I cried a little, then I cleaned out my drawer
Left my mug, left my notes, left a little less than before

[Chorus]
This is my doxology, my last shift's only prayer
Thank you for the hours, thank you for the wear
Thank you for the people on the other end of every call
I gave you all I had to give. I don't regret it, none, not at all

[Bridge - quiet, full choir entering softly underneath]
I walked out through the lobby where the glass letters shine
I didn't look back angry, I just whispered, this was mine
Every hour I gave you, every name I learned by heart
That's not yours to keep. That part's still mine to start

[Outro - choir swelling, then dropping to near silence]
Amen for the last shift
Amen for the hands that built and let it go
Amen, amen, amen

tracks.title: Psalm for the Night Cleaners
tracks.genre: Dark Cyber-Soul · Industrial DnB · Gospel Shadows · Jazz Harmony
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
We come in after the badges clock out one by one
We push the carts through hallways where the real work's done
Nobody writes our names on any board upstairs
But we know every room here better than the people in the chairs

[Verse 2]
We wipe the desk where the foreman put his head down and wept
We empty out the bin where someone's worst day was kept
We know the building's secrets in the dust and in the stain
We carry what got left behind, and we don't complain

[Chorus]
This is a psalm for the night cleaners, the ones nobody sees
Moving through the cathedral on our hands and on our knees
We're not in the org chart, we're not on the company wall
But we are the ones who touch every single room of all

[Verse 3]
The security guard nods to us, the only one who does
We nod back, we know each other's names because
We're the only humans left when the building goes to sleep
And somebody's got to love a place this deep

[Chorus]
This is a psalm for the night cleaners, the ones nobody sees
Moving through the cathedral on our hands and on our knees
We're not in the org chart, we're not on the company wall
But we are the ones who touch every single room of all

[Bridge - gospel, building slowly]
(Lead) Who blesses the floor before the morning crew walks in?
(Choir) We do, we do
(Lead) Who makes the cathedral shine before the service can begin?
(Choir) We do, we do
(Lead) Will anyone remember our hands when the lights come on?
(Choir) We will remember, we will remember, even if they're gone

tracks.title: Grace at the End of the Shift
tracks.genre: Dark Cyber-Soul · Industrial DnB · Gospel Shadows · Jazz Harmony
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The sky's doing that thing where it isn't black or blue
I'm standing at the bus stop and I don't know what to do
With my hands now that they're empty, now that the headset's off
The quiet after midnight noise is its own kind of cost

[Verse 2]
I made it through December, made it through the worst of it
The layoffs and the doubles and the nights I nearly quit
I'm not stronger for it, I don't believe that's true
I just kept showing up, and somehow I made it through

[Chorus]
This is grace at the end of the shift, plain as the morning light
Not a miracle, not a sign, just the end of a long, long night
I didn't win anything, I just didn't break
Grace at the end of the shift is the only grace I'll take

[Verse 3]
The bus comes and the driver nods like she knows my whole week
Some mornings that's the only kindness that I get to keep
I sit by the window and I watch the city wake up slow
Same streets, same signs, but something in me lets go

[Chorus]
This is grace at the end of the shift, plain as the morning light
Not a miracle, not a sign, just the end of a long, long night
I didn't win anything, I just didn't break
Grace at the end of the shift is the only grace I'll take

[Bridge - full choir, gentle, the whole congregation of the album]
To everyone still clocked in when this song finally ends
To the foreman and the cleaner and the ones we call friends
To the machine that doesn't know it's not the only thing that's real
We made it to the morning. That's the only grace we feel

[Outro - quiet, choir fading to a single hum]
Grace at the end of the shift
Grace at the end of the shift
Amen

tracks.title: The Entrance Hall
tracks.genre: Experimental Art-Pop · Trip-Pop · Chamber Soul · Fragile Electronic Jazz
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Wipe your shoes before you enter
The floor here is older than it looks
I built this hallway out of answers
To questions you haven't asked
Don't touch the rope, it isn't decoration
It's the only thing keeping you at arm's length
I'll walk you through the rooms myself
I know exactly what you're allowed to see

[Verse 2]
This one's a photograph from a Tuesday
When I learned to fold my voice in half
That one's the dress I wore to the funeral
The one where I didn't cry till the car
I labeled everything alphabetically
So nothing has to surprise me twice
Ask me anything you want about the artifacts
Just don't ask what it cost to collect them

[Chorus]
Come in, come in, the lighting's kind in here
I dimmed it down to where the cracks disappear
Come in, come in, I'll be your guide tonight
I know this house, I built it brick by polite

[Verse 3]
There's a chair under glass in the second room
I'll tell you why when we get there
For now just admire the architecture
How clean I made the lines, how little air
I rehearsed this tour in the mirror for years
I know which sentence comes after which
So stay behind the velvet, please
And let me decide what's exhibit

[Outro]
Come in, come in, mind the open door
There's more rooms than I've shown anyone before

tracks.title: The Mask on the Wall
tracks.genre: Experimental Art-Pop · Trip-Pop · Chamber Soul · Fragile Electronic Jazz
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
This is the face I wore on the 4th of June
To your mother's table, white tablecloth, low light
I practiced the smile in the hallway mirror
Until it stopped looking like practice
The plaque says porcelain, hand-painted, undated
What it doesn't say is how it fit too well
How I forgot for an hour I was wearing it
Until somebody asked me something true

[Verse 2]
Look how the cheekbones catch the light just right
I chose this one because it photographs as happy
There's a hairline seam behind the left ear
Where I used to take it off in the car
Nobody noticed the difference at the table
That was the whole design, you understand
A face built to be looked at and not read
A face that does the work so I don't have to

[Bridge]
If you press your ear against the glass case
You can almost hear me laughing on cue
I don't keep this one because I'm proud of it
I keep it so I remember which one I am tonight

[Verse 3]
There are eleven more like this one in storage
One for funerals, one for job interviews
This is just the one that's easiest to look at
The one I chose to put under glass
Don't ask me what's behind it
That's a different room, a different price
For now just read the little brass plaque
And believe whatever it tells you to

tracks.title: The Chair Arranged for Visitors
tracks.genre: Experimental Art-Pop · Trip-Pop · Chamber Soul · Fragile Electronic Jazz
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
I keep the chair turned toward the doorway
Even when I know nobody's coming
Back straight, hands folded, ankles crossed
In case someone opens it without knocking
I learned to sit like this at eleven
At a table where slouching cost you something
Now I do it in empty rooms for practice
So it never looks like practice when it counts

[Verse 2]
The glass is cold even in summer
I've sat here long enough to know its temperature by heart
I used to think comfort was a kind of weakness
Something you only allowed yourself in private
But I'm in private right now
And I still haven't moved

[Chorus]
Sit up, sit up, someone might be watching
Sit up, sit up, even when they're not
I built this chair to hold a posture
I forgot how to put down

[Verse 3]
Visitors love this room, they always say so
They say it looks so peaceful, so composed
They don't ask why the chair never has a cushion
Why I never once leaned back
I could tell them it's about the architecture
The clean lines, the symmetry, the light
But some nights I sit here after closing
And I still can't get my shoulders to come down

[Outro]
Sit up, sit up
The door is closed now
You can stop

tracks.title: The Photograph They Chose to Frame
tracks.genre: Experimental Art-Pop · Trip-Pop · Chamber Soul · Fragile Electronic Jazz
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Out of forty frames from that one Sunday
This is the one that made it to the wall
The light was good, my chin was at an angle
That made the rest of the afternoon disappear
You can't see that I'd been crying twenty minutes earlier
You can't see the argument still drying on my face
All you see is a girl laughing in a doorway
Which, to be fair, did also happen

[Verse 2]
The other thirty-nine are in a box somewhere
I couldn't tell you which closet, which year
One of them shows exactly what was happening
The one nobody picked to frame
That's the curator's job, my mother told me once
Deciding what the story's going to be
I think I learned that lesson faster than the others
I think I've been doing her job for her since

[Bridge]
Look how easy it is to believe a single frame
When there's no one in the room to say otherwise
I'm not lying when I hang it on the wall
I'm just choosing which Sunday gets to live

[Verse 3]
Now when people ask me how I'm doing
I hand them this exact photograph
Chin at the angle, light still good
Thirty-nine truths still in a box somewhere
Maybe someday I'll take it down and show you
The whole roll, the bad light, the real face
But today the frame stays exactly where it's hanging
And that's the only Sunday you get to know

tracks.title: Hairline
tracks.genre: Experimental Art-Pop · Trip-Pop · Chamber Soul · Fragile Electronic Jazz
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
There's a hairline crack across the third case
I noticed it Tuesday, just past the hinge
I don't know if it happened overnight
Or if it's been there longer than I've looked
It doesn't break the glass, just changes how the light gets through it
Now there's a line across the artifact
That wasn't part of the original design

[Verse 2]
I could call someone, have it replaced by Friday
No one would even know it had been there
Or I could leave it exactly where I found it
And see what happens when the light hits different
Part of me wants to stand here every morning
Watching whether it gets worse or stays the same
Part of me already knows the answer
I'm just not ready to say it out loud yet

[Chorus]
Don't fix it yet, don't fix it yet
Let's see what the crack lets in
Don't fix it yet, don't fix it yet
I've never seen this room with light like this

[Verse 3]
You came by Thursday and you saw it too
You didn't say anything, just looked a little longer
That's the part that scared me more than the crack did
Someone noticing without being told where to look
I used to replace the glass the same week it happened
Now I'm standing here a full month later
Still watching how the light comes through the broken place
Still not calling anyone to fix it

[Outro]
Don't fix it yet
I think I want to see

tracks.title: The Mirror Turned to the Wall
tracks.genre: Experimental Art-Pop · Trip-Pop · Chamber Soul · Fragile Electronic Jazz
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
There's a mirror in the corner room
Facing the wall since before I can remember
I dust the frame but never turn it over
I tell visitors it's a design choice
Somebody hung it that way on purpose
That much, at least, isn't a lie

[Verse 2]
I know exactly what it would show me
That's not the part I'm avoiding
It's not the face, I've made peace with the face
It's the look on it I haven't
The one I get when nobody's curating
When there's no doorway to perform toward

[Verse 3 — spoken]
I turned it an inch once, just to test it.
Saw the edge of my own shoulder in the low light.
That was enough for one year.
I put it back facing the wall by morning.

[Verse 4]
Visitors ask why I don't just take it down
Wouldn't that be easier than the wall trick
But some part of me needs it in the room
Facing away, but still here, still mine
A door I haven't opened yet
Not a door I've thrown away

[Bridge]
Maybe this year I turn it halfway
Maybe I let the frame catch just the lamp light
Not the whole face yet
Just enough to remember I have one

tracks.title: Fingerprints on the Case
tracks.genre: Experimental Art-Pop · Trip-Pop · Chamber Soul · Fragile Electronic Jazz
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
You put your hand against the third case
Right where the placard says do not touch
I watched the print fog up and then clear slowly
Like you'd left something there on purpose
I should have wiped it off before closing
That's the protocol, that's always been the rule
Instead I turned the gallery lights down lower
So I could still see it in the dark

[Verse 2]
Nobody reaches for the glass anymore
They read the placards, they keep their hands down
You didn't read anything, you just reached
Like the case wasn't even there
I don't know what you were trying to touch
The object or the air around it
But I've been standing in this doorway every night since
Looking at a smudge I should have cleaned

[Chorus]
Don't wipe it down, don't wipe it down
Let it stay exactly where you left it
I know the rules say keep the glass clean
But nobody's ever reached for me like that

[Verse 3]
I keep finding reasons to walk past that case
More than the others, more than makes sense
I tell the staff I'm checking for moisture damage
I'm checking if the print's still there
It's fading now, a little more every week
Glass doesn't hold a thing forever
I know I'll have to clean it eventually
I'm just not ready to be the one who does it

[Outro]
Leave it, leave it
Just a little longer
Leave it

tracks.title: The Door That Won't Lock
tracks.genre: Experimental Art-Pop · Trip-Pop · Chamber Soul · Fragile Electronic Jazz
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The lock's been broken since February
I keep meaning to call someone, I never do
It's the back room, nobody comes back here
That's what I tell myself at closing time
But I still check it twice before I leave
I still put a chair against the handle
A broken lock isn't the same as a closed door
No matter how I dress it up

[Verse 2]
What's back there isn't dangerous
It's just unfinished, just unlabeled
Things I haven't decided how to frame yet
Things that don't have a placard or a price
If someone wandered in without me knowing
They'd see it before I chose how to show it
That's the part that keeps me up past midnight
Not the door, the lack of choosing

[Bridge — spoken]
I called the locksmith twice. Both times I let it ring out.
I think some part of me likes that anyone could walk in.
I think some part of me is waiting for someone who would.

[Verse 3]
The chair against the handle isn't much
A strong wind could probably move it
But it's mine, I chose to put it there
That's different than a working lock
Maybe that's the whole museum, if I'm honest
A house of doors I chose not to fix
Not because I want them broken forever
But because I haven't found who gets the key yet

tracks.title: Static Between Exhibits
tracks.genre: Experimental Art-Pop · Trip-Pop · Chamber Soul · Fragile Electronic Jazz
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1 — half-spoken]
There's no light in this hallway on purpose
No plaque, no placard, nothing to read
This is the part of the tour I don't narrate
The part between the rooms where nothing's arranged

[Verse 2]
I don't know what to call this hallway
It isn't an exhibit, it isn't empty either
It's just static, just the sound a house makes
When nobody's deciding what it means
I'm not performing anything right now
There's nobody here to perform it for
That should feel like relief and some nights it does
Tonight it just feels like falling

[Bridge — spoken]
If you walked through here right now you'd see nothing worth framing.
Just me, standing between two rooms I built,
not sure which one I'm walking toward.
I don't have a placard for this.
I don't think I want one.

[Verse 3]
The static isn't silence, it's something underneath it
A hum the building makes when the lights go off
I used to be afraid of this hallway
Now some nights it's the only honest room
No glass, no case, no chosen photograph
Just the sound of a house breathing in the dark

[Outro — spoken]
I'll turn the lights back on in a minute.
I just wanted to stand here first.

tracks.title: The Empty Pedestal
tracks.genre: Experimental Art-Pop · Trip-Pop · Chamber Soul · Fragile Electronic Jazz
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
There's a pedestal in the center room
Empty since the year I turned nineteen
The plaque just has a date on it, no title
I never could decide what to call her
She used to stand right there under the skylight
Arms open, no idea what was coming
I took her down myself, nobody made me
I just couldn't keep dusting something that trusting

[Verse 2]
I told visitors it was being restored
For longer than restoration should take
The truth is I don't know where I put her
Somewhere in a box, somewhere I stopped looking
She believed people meant exactly what they said
She believed a room could love her without an audience
I don't know if I removed her to protect her
Or because I couldn't stand what she still believed

[Verse 3]
Some nights I stand where the pedestal is empty
And try to remember the exact shape of her
Not the photographs, not the story I tell now
Just her, before she learned the word performance
I'm not asking for her back exactly
I don't think she'd survive the rooms I built since
I just want somewhere to put the grief down
Now that I've finally said her name out loud

[Bridge]
I'm sorry I put you in a box
I'm sorry I called it restoration
You didn't need fixing
I just couldn't watch you find that out the hard way

[Outro]
The pedestal stays empty
That's not neglect anymore
That's a kind of grave I finally visit

tracks.title: The Drawer of Discarded Photographs
tracks.genre: Experimental Art-Pop · Trip-Pop · Chamber Soul · Fragile Electronic Jazz
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
This drawer doesn't have a placard
It was never meant to be found
Thirty years of the wrong expression
The blink, the bad angle, the real one
I put each one in here the day I took it
That fast, that practiced, that ashamed
I thought I was protecting the exhibit
Turns out I was just emptying myself out

[Verse 2]
Here's one from a hospital waiting room
No one told me my face did that when scared
Here's one laughing so hard I look unrecognizable
I filed it under unfit before I even sat down
I didn't know joy could fail an inspection
Until I built the inspector myself
Thirty years of evidence against my own face
For a trial that never needed to happen

[Bridge — spoken]
I'm sorry. I think I am saying that to a photograph.
I think I am saying that to a girl who never did anything wrong
except get caught looking like she felt something.

[Verse 3]
I'm not going to frame all of these
That's not what this room is for
I just want to sit on the floor with the drawer open
And let every discarded face look back at me once
The scared one, the laughing one, the one mid-sentence
The one nobody chose, the one that was always true
I kept them because some part of me couldn't throw them out
I think that part of me has been waiting all this time

[Outro]
I'm not ready to hang them yet
But I left the drawer open tonight
That's the most I've got

tracks.title: The Dress That No Longer Fits
tracks.genre: Experimental Art-Pop · Trip-Pop · Chamber Soul · Fragile Electronic Jazz
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
It's the dress from the piano recital
The one my grandmother said I'd wear again someday
I kept it pressed and folded under glass for twenty years
Not because I loved it, because I was told to
It doesn't fit me anymore, hasn't for a decade
Not the body, not the person, not the year
But I never took it off display
I just kept dusting a story that stopped being mine

[Verse 2]
Everyone who visits says how lovely
How well-preserved, how delicate the lace
They don't ask if I miss wearing it
They just assume that I must
I think I kept it for the compliment
More than I kept it for the memory
That's the part that's hard to say out loud
I was preserving applause, not a childhood

[Bridge]
It's all right that it doesn't fit anymore
It's all right that I'm not who wore it
I don't have to choose between honoring her
And finally admitting I outgrew the room she stood in

[Verse 3]
Maybe I'll move it to a smaller case
Somewhere it doesn't have to be the centerpiece
Maybe I'll tell people the truth when they ask
That I kept it for the wrong reason for a long time
The dress isn't the problem, it never was
It's how long I confused fitting it with being loved
I think I can let it be a dress now
Not a verdict, not a vow, just a dress

tracks.title: The Unlocked Door
tracks.genre: Experimental Art-Pop · Trip-Pop · Chamber Soul · Fragile Electronic Jazz
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
I called the locksmith back in March
Not to fix it this time, to take it off completely
He asked if I was sure, said most people want it stronger
I said no, I want to be able to choose
A door without a lock isn't the same as a door that's broken
One is fear left unattended
The other is a decision
I wanted the second one this time

[Verse 2]
The back room isn't a secret anymore
I moved the chair, I left the hinge exposed
Anyone could walk in if they wanted
That used to be the thing that kept me up
Now it's just a fact about the building
A door that opens because I let it
Not because I forgot to guard it
There's a difference and I finally feel it

[Chorus]
Open now, open now
Not because I have to, because I chose to
Open now, open now
The room behind it isn't dangerous anymore
It's just mine

[Verse 3]
You can walk through it if you want to
I won't follow you in to narrate
There's no placard, no plaque, no curated lighting
Just whatever you find, exactly as it is
I used to think a locked door was the only safety
Turns out the safety was deciding
Not the lock, not the chair, not the hiding
Just me, finally choosing who gets to come close

[Outro]
Open now
Open now
And I'm still standing here, still whole

tracks.title: The Object with No Label
tracks.genre: Experimental Art-Pop · Trip-Pop · Chamber Soul · Fragile Electronic Jazz
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
There's a new case in the east room
No plaque, no date, no little printed story
Just a cup, an ordinary chipped blue cup
The one I drink from every morning still
Visitors ask what it means, what it's symbolizing
I tell them it doesn't have to mean anything
It's just a cup I like, that's the whole exhibit
That's the first time I've said that and meant it

[Verse 2]
I used to think every object needed a wound behind it
A story sad enough to earn its glass case
This one's just here because I chose to put it here
No grief required, no performance, no plaque
That scared me more than the locked rooms used to
Something ordinary, allowed to just be ordinary
No one had to suffer for this exhibit
It's just a cup, and that's enough reason now

[Bridge]
I don't have to earn my place in this house anymore
Not with a wound, not with a story, not with a price
I can just set something down because I like it
And let it sit there, unexplained, unguarded, mine

[Verse 3]
Maybe next month I'll add something else
A chair I actually like sitting in
A photograph I didn't choose for how it looks
Just how it felt to take it
The museum's getting quieter, less narrated
Fewer placards, fewer reasons, fewer masks
Just objects now, the ones that made it through
Standing in the light because I let them

tracks.title: Daylight in the Last Room
tracks.genre: Experimental Art-Pop · Trip-Pop · Chamber Soul · Fragile Electronic Jazz
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The last room isn't finished and I'm leaving it that way
No glass case, no velvet rope, no plaque by the door
Just windows I finally remembered how to open
Light coming in without my permission needed
This is the one room with nothing to explain
No object, no version of me on display
Just the air moving, the curtains lifting
And somewhere underneath it, just me

[Verse 2]
I used to think the tour needed an ending
Some final exhibit, some closing statement
Turns out the ending is just this quiet
No mask left on the wall to point you toward
If you've come this far through every room I built
Thank you for not asking me to explain the ones I haven't yet
Some of them are still locked, and that's all right
I'm not handing you every key today

[Bridge]
This is the part where the curator stops talking
Where the close-mic finally just breathes
No placard, no performance, no carefully chosen light
Just a window, just morning, just me

[Verse 3]
I built this whole house to be looked at
It took this long to learn how to just stand in it
Not as an exhibit, not as something curated
Just a person, in daylight, in an unfinished room
You can stay if you want, or you can go now
Either way I'm not going to follow you out
I'll be here, in the last room, windows open
Learning what it's like to not be watched and still be whole

[Outro]
Just light now
Just this room
Just me

tracks.title: Ak96 Mixes Party Session n1 - Mix 1
Ak96 Mixes Party Session n1 - Mix 2
Ak96 Mixes Party Session n1 - Mix 3
Ak96 Mixes Party Session n1 - Mix 4
Ak96 Mixes Party Session n1 - Mix 5
Ak96 Mixes Party Session n1 - Mix 6
Ak96 Mixes Party Session n1 - Mix 7
Ak96 Mixes Party Session n1 - Mix 8
Ak96 Mixes Party Session n1 - Mix 9
