Ak96 Mixes Party Session n1 - Mix 11
The House We Couldn't Leave
tracks.filename: hd_10 - The House We Couldn't Leave.wav
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
You found me staring at the ceiling again Three in the morning, talking to ghosts in
My head The television flickered blue on the walls
Like a lighthouse for ships that were already lost
I said I was fine, you knew I was lying But neither of us wanted to start that fight

So I disappeared into longer nights Into crowded rooms and city lights
Trying to outrun things I couldn't name I lit little fires all over the house
Just to see who would stay I broke every window I could find
Just to keep the daylight away But no matter how hard I ran

No matter how far I went There you were
Like you always are The hardest truths never make a sound
They live in the spaces between what comes out That I'm tired, they're not tonight, that nothing's wrong
Repeated a thousand times I thought if I carried the weight alone

Nobody else would have to feel it But all I did was make the distance grow
And every road I took to disappear Somehow led me back here
I lit little fires all over the house Just to see who would stay
I broke every window I could find Just to keep the daylight away

But no matter how hard I ran No matter how far I went
There you were Like you always are
Maybe family isn't blood Maybe it isn't names
Maybe it's the people who stand in the rain While you're screaming at the sky

Trying to convince the stars You don't need saving
And I know, I know I've made it hard
I've made it hard To love me sometimes
I lit little fires all over the house And watched the smoke fill every room

I thought if I pushed you far enough You'd finally leave town
But when the walls came down And the embers died
You were standing there On the other side
Waiting Like you always were

The house is quiet now The ghosts don't talk as much
And for the first time I think I'm ready
To tell the truth

albums.title: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn

tracks.title: Why
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
When I was younger, I thought wisdom was an answer
A destination hidden somewhere beyond the next horizon
I thought if I walked far enough, worked hard enough, learned enough
One day everything would make sense

But every door I opened led to another hallway
And every truth I found gave birth to another lie
Why, why do we love, knowing one day we'll have to let go
Why, why do we build when nothing in this world can stay

Why, why does a moment disappear while a memory lasts forever
Why, why
Why does time move faster the older that we get
Why do some faces fade while others never leave us

Why does a song carry us somewhere a thousand words never could
Why does kindness matter when nobody sees it
The older I get, the less I seem to know
And strangely, the more beautiful that becomes

Why, why do we search for things that were already there
Why, why do we spend years running back to the places we began
Why, why do the people we love change us forever
Why

Maybe the stars don't know our senses
Maybe the ocean keeps its secrets
Maybe the meaning isn't hidden at the end of the road
Maybe the meaning is the road itself

And maybe, maybe, maybe that's enough
Why, why does life feel so fragile and so magnificent at the same time
Why, why do ordinary days become the ones we miss the most
Why, why do we keep loving even when it hurts

Why, why, why, why
When I was young, I thought wisdom meant answers
Now I think wisdom is learning how to live with the questions

tracks.title: Who Wins If I Win
tracks.filename: hd_2 - Who Wins if I win.wav
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
Who wins if I win
I spent half my life chasing horizons
Running toward names on distant doors
Trading comfort for another promise

Always believing there was something more
I crossed cities, I crossed oceans
I crossed years I can't reclaim
Always staring at the mountain

Never asking who helped me climb the same
Now the road is getting quieter
And the nights are getting long
So I find myself returning

To a question all along
Who wins if I win
Is it the man in the mirror
Or the hands that pulled me up

Whenever the road got steeper
Who wins if I win
The hearts that carried mine
The souls that paid in silence

So I could chase this life
Tell me
When the dust settles in
Who wins if I win

My father taught me courage
My mother taught me grace
Friends became my shelter
Whenever I lost my place

The woman standing beside me
Carried more than I could see
While I was building distant futures
She was building me

Every lesson, every failure
Every scar beneath my skin
Feels a little less like mine now
When I think of where they've been

Who wins if I win
The dream was never mine alone
Every mile I traveled
Was paid by those back home

Who wins if I win
The voices through the years
The ones who stood beside me
Through every doubt and fear

Tell me
When the dust settles in
Who wins if I win
Maybe nobody wins alone

Maybe nobody ever did
Every victory is stitched together
From a thousand invisible gifts
A teacher, a friend, a sacrifice

A door held open at the perfect time
A hand on your shoulder when you almost gave up
A voice saying
One more try

The world will know my name
But they won't know the story
Of all the quiet heroes
Standing behind the glory

And if I leave this world
With anything worth giving
Let it be this truth
That no one climbs alone

Who wins if I win
My children and their future
The people who believed
Before there was proof

The ones who never asked
For credit or applause
The ones who gave their love
Without keeping score

Who wins if I win
The answer's finally clear
Every person who walked beside me
Brought me here

And when the final chapter closes
And the lights grow dim
I'll know
Nobody wins alone

Nobody ever did
So if I stand upon a mountain
Looking back at where I've been
The greatest view was never mine

It belonged to all of them

tracks.title: The Roots We Cannot See
tracks.filename: hd_3 - The Roots We Cannot See.wav
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
The world admires the tallest branches Reaching proudly toward the sky
The leaves that dance in golden sunlight The beauty visible to every eye
But nobody stops to wonder What keeps that giant standing there
Through every storm, through every winter Year after year

Because the strongest things in life Are rarely seen at all
We admire the branches But it's the roots we cannot see
The quiet years The countless moments that become our history
The storms we weather The tears we dry

The reasons we stayed when we could have said goodbye
That's where the strength lives That's where the truth will be
And the roots we cannot see
A friendship isn't built in laughter Though laughter helps along the way

It's built in all the days between When life gets hard and people stay
A family isn't made by blood alone Nor promises spoken easily
It's built in every act of kindness Nobody else will ever see
And every year adds another ring To who we choose to be

We admire the branches But it's the roots we cannot see
The phone calls answered The doors left open to shared uncertainty
The times we failed The times we forgave
The reasons we stayed when we could have walked away

That's where the strength lives That's where the truth will be
And the roots we cannot see
One day the leaves will fall One day the seasons change
One day the world may forget our names But the love we planted, the trust we grew

Will still be holding everything long after we're gone
Because magnificent things are never built in moments
They are built in years, in patience, in loyalty
In choosing each other again and again

We admire the branches Stretching higher every year
But every beautiful thing above Began somewhere down here
And the roots we cannot see
The sacrifices, the faith, the time The people who became our home

By standing through every season of our lives
The strongest part of any tree is hidden underground
Just like love, just like family
Just like us

tracks.title: If We Can't Say The Hard Truths
tracks.filename: hd_4 - If We Can't Say The Hard Truths.wav
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
The kitchen light was the only thing awake Half-drunk coffee, two shadows, one table
The city was sleeping somewhere beyond the glass
While we sat there, carefully avoiding the things that mattered
Talking about weather, talking about work Talking round the wound like circling a fire

Neither of us willing to step through the smoke
And I watched you searching for safer words
While mine stayed buried beneath the dirt
But if we can't say the hard truths, then what the hell are we doing here

What's the point of all these years, of all these nights, of all these tears
If I can't hand you the pieces that I'm afraid to let you see
Then tell me, what kind of love is this supposed to be
We've become experts at surviving conversations

Masters of changing subjects, professional escape artists
Smiling at the perfect moment, laughing at the right lines
Building entire cities out of things we never say
But silence grows like ivy on a wall

And one day, it owns it all
If we can't say the hard truths, then what the hell are we doing here
What's the point of all these years, of all these nights, of all these fears
If I can't bring you the darkness and trust you'll stay with me

Then tell me, what kind of love is this supposed to be
Maybe honesty isn't beautiful
Maybe it arrives shaking, voice cracking, hands trembling
Maybe courage isn't speaking loudly

Maybe courage is whispering, I need help
I miss you, I'm scared, I was wrong
I love you, before it's too late
Built because life is shorter than we keep pretending

And hearts break from the words they never hear
More than the words they do
So let's say the hard truths
Let's drag them into the light

Let's stop pretending everything's alright
Let's risk the awkward silence
Let's risk the pain, let's risk the storm instead of the distance
If we're going to share this life, then let's really be here

Because if we can't say the hard truths, then what the hell are we doing here
The coffee went cold, the city woke up
And for the first time, neither of us looked away

tracks.title: Still Worth It
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
People told me I was foolish For believing what they said
For opening my door too quickly For giving more than I ever kept
They said I'd learn eventually That kindness only leaves a mark
That the world belongs to sinners And soft hearts break apart

Maybe they're right Maybe I've paid the price
More than once, more than twice
I've been fooled, I've been used I've been disappointed too
But if helping ten people only reaches one That's true, then I'd do it all again

Without a second thought Because kindness has a price
And I know exactly what it costs It's still worth it
I've watched promises unravel I've watched trusts disappear
I've watched people take a hit And then vanish when the road got steep

And every scar reminds me Of a lesson life revealed
But not a single one convinced me To stop believing people need help
Because somewhere out there Someone's carrying the weight
They were never meant to carry alone

I've been fooled, I've been used I've been disappointed too
But if helping ten people only reaches one That's true, then I'd do it all again
Without a second thought Because kindness has a price
And I know exactly what it costs It's still worth it

I refuse to let the worst people I meet Decide how I treat the best ones
I refuse to let betrayal become wisdom Or disappointment become who I am
The world gave me reasons to close every door I just chose not to build
Not because I'm blind Not because I'm naive

Not because I expect anything back But because when I look in the mirror
I wanna recognize the man looking back
Oh, I've been fooled, I've been used I've been broken here and there
But if someone truly needs me I still hope I'll be there

I'd rather carry the scars Than lose the part of me
That still believes people matter That still believes in decency
Because kindness has a price And I've paid it more than once
It's still worth it It's still worth it

It's still worth it The cost was real
The lesson was real But so was the reason
I helped in the first place

tracks.title: Side by Side
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
I've shaken hands with people whose names are on the door I've shared a table with dreamers
Who were sleeping on the floor And the years have taught me something
That money never could The best people I've ever known
Were simply doing good Because titles pay

And fortunes change Faster than we realize
Side by side, that's where people belong Not above, not below, just walking along
Your title tells me what you do It doesn't tell me who you are
So I'll meet you where we started Human heart to human heart

Side by side I've met kings inside offices
And kings without a crown I've met people with everything
Who still look broken down And I've met people with nothing
Who carried endless light That's when I learned that character

Outshines status every time Because respect was never meant
To be reserved for a few Side by side, that's where people belong
Not above, not below, just walking along Your title tells me what you do
It doesn't tell me who you are So I'll meet you where we started

Human heart to human heart Side by side
One day you'll meet people Who want you to feel small
Because standing on others Makes them feel taller
Don't learn that lesson, don't carry that weight

The strongest people I've ever known Never needed to prove they were great
Look people in the eyes Listen before you speak
You'll learn more from kindness Than you'll ever learn from prestige
Side by side, that's where people belong Not above, not below, just walking along

The richest man, the newest hire The famous and unknown
Every single one of us Just wants a place to call home
Side by side
Side by side

Side by side
Titles tell a story But never the whole truth
The rest is written

tracks.title: As Long as You're Okay
tracks.filename: hd_7 - As long as you're okay.wav
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
inspiration
Everybody hides something
Behind a smile, behind a face
Little storms nobody notices

Silent battles every day
We carry them like secrets
Locked away where no one sees
Pretending that we're stronger

Than we really need to be
But tonight, I don't need the answers
I don't need to explain
As long as you're okay

Maybe I'll be okay too
I can carry what I'm carrying
If the light still shines on you
The world can keep its questions

The sky can keep its rain
I don't need everything to be perfect
As long as you're okay
There's a secret we all keep

The one we never say out loud
How sometimes we're just surviving
Trying to make our people proud
And every heart is holding stories

That words can never quite explain
But somehow seeing you smile
Makes them easier to contain
And tonight, that's enough

That's enough for me
As long as you're okay
Maybe I'll be okay too
I can carry what I'm carrying

If the light still shines on you
The world can keep its questions
The sky can keep its rain
I don't need everything to be perfect

As long as you're okay
Maybe love is something simple
Maybe love is something small
Not fixing every broken piece

Just being there through it all
Not having all the answers
Not taking all the pain away
Just looking at someone you love

And hoping they're okay
As long as you're okay
Maybe I'll be okay too
The road can turn, the years can pass

The seasons can break through
The world can keep its questions
The sky can keep its rain
I don't need everything to be perfect

As long as you're okay
There's a secret we all keep
And maybe that's okay

tracks.title: It Was Already There
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
I spent years chasing distant answers Across cities, oceans, and skies
Believing somewhere beyond the horizon There was a truth I hadn't found
I searched in books, I searched in people I searched in places I barely knew
Always convinced the missing piece was waiting somewhere new

But every road I followed, every lesson I survived Kept leading me back to something I couldn't quite describe
It was already there Beneath the noise, beneath the fear, beneath the years
I thought I was searching for something rare But every answer I ever needed, it was already there
I look for strength in distant mountains For wisdom in older names

For certainty in perfect plans For peace inside achievement
I thought becoming someone Would finally make me whole
Not knowing all along The missing map was written in my soul
And every time life broke me, every time I lost my way Something quiet inside me still knew what to say

It was already there The light beneath the dark
The hope beneath the scars The fire beneath the spark
I thought I was finding what wasn't anywhere But every truth worth keeping, it was already there
The love I was looking for Was hidden in familiar faces

The courage I was searching for Was born in the hardest places
The home I missed Was never made of walls
It lived inside the people Who answered when I called
Maybe we spent half our lives searching And the other half understanding

Maybe the treasure was never buried Maybe we were standing on it all along
It was already there In every lesson, in every fall
In every hand that caught me When I thought I'd lost it all
It was already there Before I knew its name

Waiting patiently inside me Through every loss and change
And now I see The thing I spent my life pursuing
Was walking beside me the whole time
I wasn't finding anything

I was remembering
What was already there

tracks.title: Always In Your Corner
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
There'll be nights when the road disappears And every mile feels longer than the years
When your own reflection turns away And you don't recognize the face
You think you've gone too far this time Crossed some invisible line
That nobody could understand You'll be wrong

There'll be days when the words come sharp When anger speaks before the heart
When mistakes leave marks like rain on stone And you carry them alone
You'll tell yourself you've broken things Beyond what hands can mend
And you'll be wrong again Because love was never built for perfect days

It was built for this
When the lights go out, when the walls cave in When the whole world tells you where you've been
I'll still be here, like I've always been In your corner, in your corner
You don't have to win, you don't have to prove anything to me To deserve this room

I'll still be here, like I've always been In your corner
Maybe one day you'll understand The things I never found the words to say
That some people love like open doors Even after storms, even after years
Even after silence built So fall apart if you need to

Lose your way if you must stay The light left on the chair that still pulled out
The voice that says come in
When the lights go out, when the sky comes down When you're standing in the wreckage
Looking around I'll still be here, like I've always been In your corner, in your corner

Not because you're perfect, not because you're strong Not because you've never done anything wrong
I'll still be here, until the river meets the sea Until these old bones leave me
In your corner, one day when I'm gone And you're wondering if you were enough
Remember this, you never had to earn my love You already had it

tracks.title: The House We Couldn't Leave
tracks.filename: hd_10 - The House We Couldn't Leave.wav
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
You found me staring at the ceiling again Three in the morning, talking to ghosts in
My head The television flickered blue on the walls
Like a lighthouse for ships that were already lost
I said I was fine, you knew I was lying But neither of us wanted to start that fight

So I disappeared into longer nights Into crowded rooms and city lights
Trying to outrun things I couldn't name I lit little fires all over the house
Just to see who would stay I broke every window I could find
Just to keep the daylight away But no matter how hard I ran

No matter how far I went There you were
Like you always are The hardest truths never make a sound
They live in the spaces between what comes out That I'm tired, they're not tonight, that nothing's wrong
Repeated a thousand times I thought if I carried the weight alone

Nobody else would have to feel it But all I did was make the distance grow
And every road I took to disappear Somehow led me back here
I lit little fires all over the house Just to see who would stay
I broke every window I could find Just to keep the daylight away

But no matter how hard I ran No matter how far I went
There you were Like you always are
Maybe family isn't blood Maybe it isn't names
Maybe it's the people who stand in the rain While you're screaming at the sky

Trying to convince the stars You don't need saving
And I know, I know I've made it hard
I've made it hard To love me sometimes
I lit little fires all over the house And watched the smoke fill every room

I thought if I pushed you far enough You'd finally leave town
But when the walls came down And the embers died
You were standing there On the other side
Waiting Like you always were

The house is quiet now The ghosts don't talk as much
And for the first time I think I'm ready
To tell the truth

tracks.title: I Never Knew Any Other Way
tracks.filename: hd_11 - I Never Knew any other way.wav
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
I grew up watching quiet people doing what they said they'd do
Never needing recognition, never chasing someone else's truth
They shake a hand and keep a promise, look you straight in the eyes
And somehow in those simple moments, they taught me how to live my life

I didn't know they were teaching, I thought that's just how people were
I never knew any other way, to me a heart's a heart at the end of the day
I met people with nothing, and people with everything, and the best of them all
Never needed a crown to make themselves seen, I never knew any other way

I sat beside important people, and people nobody knew
And after all these years, I still believe the same is true
The loudest voices in the room, rarely leave the deepest mark
The people who change your life, usually do it in the dark

Maybe that's old-fashioned, maybe that's okay
I never knew any other way, to me a heart's a heart at the end of the day
I met people with nothing, and people with everything, and the best of them all
Never needed a crown to make themselves seen, I never knew any other way

The people who raised me, never taught me to look up or down, only eye to eye
And maybe that's the reason, no title ever changed my mind, about who matters in this life
When all the noise is over, when all the names fade away
The only thing that really stays, is how you make people feel

I never knew any other way, to me a heart's a heart at the end of the day
The richest man, the newest hire, the famous and unknown
Every single one of us just wants a place, to call home
I never knew any other way, I never knew any other way

Some lessons are spoken, some lessons are lived
The best ones become part of you
Thanks for watching

tracks.title: Leave A Light On
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
I know you'll have your own roads, your own storms to outrun
You'll chase a thousand sunsets before your day is done
You'll make mistakes like I did. You'll learn what I learned wrong
And you'll find your own reasons to keep moving on

But if the night feels colder than you thought it'd be
And all the maps you've trusted lead nowhere you can see
Leave a light on, leave a light on
If the road gets long and you can't find home

Leave a light on, leave a light on
You don't walk alone no matter where you've gone
You'll meet people worth keeping and some you'll leave behind
You'll lose things you once treasured. You'll change your heart and mind

You'll build something worth fighting for. You'll carry scars with pride
And one day you'll understand why I stood by your side
And if the world feels heavier than anyone can know
Just remember there's a doorway with a light still glowing

Leave a light on, leave a light on
If the road gets long and you can't find home
Leave a light on, leave a light on
You don't walk alone no matter where you've gone

Not for today, not for tomorrow
Maybe years from now when life gets hard to follow
You'll remember what I meant all along
Leave a light on, leave a light on

For the days you feel strong and the nights you don't
Leave a light on, leave a light on
There's a place for you there always was, there always will be
Leave a light on

The door is open, the fire is warm, the light is on
Come home
Leave a light on

tracks.title: The Empty Chair
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
There's a chair beside the window where the morning sunlight falls
Nobody has moved it lately, nobody at all
Sometimes when the house is quiet and the coffee starts to steam
I still catch myself expecting you to walk into the scene

Funny how heart remembers little things the ears can't hide
And I still leave a place for you
Even though you're not around
Sometimes love stays at the table long after it sits down

There's an empty chair beside me but somehow you're still here
In the stories, in the laughter, in the reasons I still care
There's a road we used to travel just to see the autumn leaves
Now I drive it every once in a while and feel you in the breeze

People think that time erases everything we leave behind
But the best parts of a person settle softly in your mind
Funny how heart remembers what the world forgets to see
And I still leave a place for you

Even though you're not around
Sometimes love stays at the table long after it sits down
There's an empty chair beside me but somehow you're still here
In the stories, in the laughter, in the reasons I still care

Not every goodbye is broken, not every ending has to fall apart
Some people stay forever living quietly inside your heart
And every lesson that you gave me still finds me when I'm lost
Like a lantern in the darkness, like a map I never tossed

So I'll always leave a place for you, no matter where I go
Because love is more than presents, it's the things we come to know
There's an empty chair beside me but I don't feel alone
You're the voice inside the silence, you're the feeling of home

The chair is empty but the love is not
And some things never leave, they simply change their spot

tracks.title: Good Things Grow Slow
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
Everybody's running somewhere Everybody's chasing gold
Lookin' for a shortcut highway Tryin' not to go
I've been down that road before I've been fast and I've been wrong
Now I'm sittin' on the front porch Watchin' summer come along

Funny how the little things take the longest time
Good things grow slow That's what I've come to know
The strongest trees don't rush where they go
Love takes time, so just let it go

Good things grow slow Good things grow slow
I've seen gardens turn to forests I've seen children learn to fly
One day they're holding your finger Next day they're waving goodbye
And the moments that you treasure Never happen when you chase

They arrive without a warning And leave a smile upon your face
Good things grow slow That's what I've come to know
The strongest trees don't rush where they go
Love takes time, so just let it go

Good things grow slow Good things grow slow
So take a breath And watch the sunset fall
The world keeps turning anyway You don't have to run at all
Good things grow slow That's what I've come to know

The best days come when you stop stealing
Tomorrow love takes time So does every soul
Good things grow slow Good things grow slow
The river never hurries The mountains never raise

Somehow they still get there

tracks.title: Maybe I Was Wrong
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
I spent years collecting answers Like they were trophies on a shelf
Always trying to be the smartest guy In every room I found myself
I had reasons for my reasons And a speech for every fight
But somewhere along the highway I stopped needing to be right

Funny what you learn When you stop trying to win
Maybe I was wrong, maybe that's okay Maybe growing up means changing
What you thought you'd never change Maybe all the best lessons don't arrive too
Strong Maybe I got wiser the day I said

Maybe I was wrong
I've apologized to strangers I've apologized to friends
I've apologized to people I never thought I'd understand
And every time I swallow pride The world got a little wide

Turns out humility opens doors Ego keeps locked inside
Maybe I was wrong, maybe that's okay Maybe growing up means changing
What you thought you'd never change Maybe all the best lessons don't arrive too
Strong Maybe I got wiser the day I said

Maybe I was wrong
Maybe I was wrong
The mountains don't argue The rivers don't defend
They just keep moving forward Again and again

Maybe I was wrong, maybe that's alright Maybe some of the brightest roads appear
When you stop the fight Maybe life gets better when you don't need
To be strong Maybe I became myself the day I said
Maybe I was wrong

And maybe that's where wisdom starts
Thanks for watching

tracks.title: Borrowed Time
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
I used to think there'd always be another summer down the road
Another song to learn by heart
Another story to be told
I thought the years move slowly like clouds across the sky

Funny how they start to run
The older that you get in life
So pull up one more chair and stay a little while
We're all living on borrowed time

So let's make tonight worth remembering
Raise a glass, tell another story, sing another line
We're all living on borrowed time
So don't save your laughter for another day

The clock keeps moving anyway
We're all living on borrowed time
I've watched children become dreamers
I've watched dreamers become old

I've watched people chase forever
Things they never got to hold
But the moments that stay with you never seem to cost a dime
They're usually found in conversations on borrowed time

We're all living on borrowed time
So let's make tonight worth remembering
Raise a glass, tell another story, sing another line
We're all living on borrowed time

So don't save your laughter for another day
The clock keeps moving anyway
We're all living on borrowed time
Not one of us can stop the river

Not one of us can slow the sun
So maybe the secret isn't holding on
Maybe it's loving while it's here
We're all living on borrowed time

So come sit closer to the fire

We're all living on borrowed time
And maybe that's the reason these little moments shine

We're all living on borrowed time

tracks.title: Free Men Tell the Truth
tracks.filename: hd_17 - Free Men Tell the truth.wav
tracks.album: The Life Lessons I Hope You Learn
tracks.lyrics:
Oh, there was a boy in a small town who learned to keep his head down
He learned some doors stayed open if you never made a sound
You watch good people whisper things they wish that they could say
But fear has a way of growing when the truth gets pushed away

Every secret gets heavier
Every shadow gets longer
Three men tell the truth, even when the room turns cold
Even when their hands are shaking

Even when they stand alone
Because chains aren't always iron
Sometimes they're made of fear
Three men tell the truth, and suddenly the sky gets clear

There was a woman by the river who carried a burden for years
She smiled for every photograph and hid the rest in tears
One day she spoke her story, no anger and no blame
And the weight she carried quietly never felt the same again

Funny how the hardest words can open locked doors
Three men tell the truth, even when the room turns cold
Even when their hands are shaking
Even when they stand alone

Because chains aren't always iron
Sometimes they're made of fear
Three men tell the truth, and suddenly the sky gets clear
The sky gets clear

Not to win, not to wound, not to prove that they are right
But because the truth was never meant to spend its whole life hiding
A bird was made to fly
A river for the sea

And the truth was made for daylight
The way we were made to be free
Three men tell the truth, even when it costs them comfort
Even when the road gets lonely

Even when the night feels long
Because freedom isn't getting everything you want
Freedom is having nothing left to hide from anyone
Three men tell the truth, and walk a little lighter

Three men tell the truth, and sleep a little better
Three men tell the truth, and leave the world a little brighter
The truth may cost you comfort, but a lie will cost you peace
And peace is too precious to live without

tracks.title: Blood on the Hallelujah
tracks.lyrics:
There was blood on the hallelujah
There was fire in the hymn
There was a man at the edge of himself
Trying to crawl back in

I had broken every promise
I had polished every chain
I had turned my pride to music
Just to dance around the pain

There were angels in the bassline
There were devils in the strings
There was a choir in the alley
Singing truth has heavier wings

Blood on the hallelujah
Smoke around the prayer
I was looking for forgiveness
But forgiveness found me there

Blood on the hallelujah
Light behind the bruise
I had nothing left to offer
So I offered up the truth

I had worn my shame like velvet
I had called it being strong
I had made a little palace
From the places I was wrong

But the night became a witness
And the walls began to breathe
And every song I ever buried
Started rising underneath

There was no clean resurrection
There was no white shining road
Just a man with empty pockets
And a heart that almost broke

Blood on the hallelujah
Smoke around the prayer
I was looking for forgiveness
But forgiveness found me there

Blood on the hallelujah
Light behind the bruise
I had nothing left to offer
So I offered up the truth

Tell my mother I was frightened
Tell my father I was proud
Tell my lover I was lonely
But I could not say it loud

Tell the children if they ask me
Why the good men fall apart
Sometimes God comes like a rhythm
To break open the heart

Blood on the hallelujah
But I’m singing anyway
With my knees in the ashes
And my hands above the flames

Blood on the hallelujah
Let the old life come unglued
I had nothing left to offer
So I offered up the truth

tracks.title: It's not always easy
tracks.lyrics:
I did not find you waiting
At the end of some golden road
You were already in the weather
With your sleeves rolled to the bone

You were there when the rooms were empty
When the floorboards knew our names
When the future was a candle
And the nights were full of rain

I have seen you make a kingdom
Out of bread, light, and tired hands
I have watched you hold the morning
Like it came because you planned

And I was not always easy
I was not always wise
But you kept a place beside me
Where the better man survived

You are the woman beside the fire
The quiet architect of my days
The one who made a home from nothing
And never asked the world for praise

You are the hand inside the thunder
The roof when the sky came through
If they ask me what I built here
I will say I built it next to you

Next to you
I built it next to you

There were bills on the table
There were dreams we had to fold
There were winters in the hallway
There were stories left untold

But you laughed in the kitchen
And the walls remembered spring
You could turn an ordinary evening
Into an almost sacred thing

I chased roads and distant windows
I wore distance like a coat
You kept love inside the house
Like a lamp, like a boat

And every time I came back tired
From the noise, from the race
There was mercy in your silence
There was daylight on your face

You are the woman beside the fire
The quiet architect of my days
The one who made a home from nothing
And never asked the world for praise

You are the hand inside the thunder
The roof when the sky came through
If they ask me what I built here
I will say I built it next to you

Next to you
I built it next to you

The bass moves under midnight
Like a heart beneath the floor
The violins rise softly
Like someone opening a door

And there you are, still standing
Not polished, not pretending
More beautiful than the promise
Because you stayed through the bending

I do not need a monument
I do not need my name in stone
I have seen your shadow near mine
And I know I was not alone

All the things I thought were mine
Were never mine to claim
You were the hidden river
Running under everything

You are the woman beside the fire
The quiet architect of my days
The one who made a home from nothing
And never asked the world for praise

You are the hand inside the thunder
The roof when the sky came through
If they ask me what I built here
I will say I built it next to you

Next to you
I built it next to you

If the years take down the garden
If the paint falls from the door
If the children find their own skies
And do not need us like before

I will still know where to find you
In the small light after rain
In the cup beside the window
In the music of your name

Not every love is lightning
Some love is wood and flame
Some love holds the whole house up
And never signs its name

You are the woman beside the fire
The quiet architect of my days
The one who made a home from nothing
And never asked the world for praise

You are the hand inside the thunder
The roof when the sky came through
If they ask me what I built here
I will say I built it next to you

Next to you
I built it next to you

Everything I ever became
Had your fingerprints in blue
If I have a life worth singing
I built it next to you

Next to you
I built it next to you

tracks.title: Keep a Chair for you
tracks.lyrics:
I know a little table
Where the lamps forgive the rain
Where the piano drinks the silence
And gives it back again

There is smoke above the glasses
There is brass behind the wall
There is someone tuning strings
Like a prayer before the fall

You came in without a thunder
No grand entrance, no disguise
Just the soft Atlantic weather
Resting quietly in your eyes

And all the rooms inside me
That I had kept half-closed
Turned their chairs toward the doorway
Like the heart already knows

Lisbon keeps a chair for you
By the river, by the rain
Every light along the water
Leans to hear me say your name

Lisbon keeps a chair for you
In the blue behind the gold
Where the night becomes a singer
And the streets forget the cold

I do not need a kingdom
I do not need a view
There is a whole life waiting
In a chair beside you

The bass walks through the room
Like an old friend coming home
The drummer brushes midnight
Into silver, skin, and bone

The guitar begins to tremble
With that sorrow we do not cure
Not the sadness that destroys us
But the one that keeps us pure

You laughed into your wine glass
And the city changed its key
Like the moon had moved the river
Just to make a place for me

I have chased too many mornings
With my coat against the wind
But your hand upon the table
Brought the weather back within

Lisbon keeps a chair for you
By the river, by the rain
Every light along the water
Leans to hear me say your name

Lisbon keeps a chair for you
In the blue behind the gold
Where the night becomes a singer
And the streets forget the cold

I do not need a kingdom
I do not need a view
There is a whole life waiting
In a chair beside you

Some love comes dressed in lightning
Some love wants a crown
But ours came through the kitchen
And gently sat me down

Ours came through the window
With the smell of morning bread
With your shoulder near my shoulder
And the words we never said

There are violins rising
Like birds above the roofs
There is gospel in the distance
There is jazz inside the truth

And the brass begins to open
Like a door inside the blue
And the whole night turns its body
Just to make more room for you

Lisbon keeps a chair for you
By the river, by the rain
Every light along the water
Leans to hear me say your name

Lisbon keeps a chair for you
In the blue behind the gold
Where the night becomes a singer
And the streets forget the cold

I do not need a kingdom
I do not need a view
There is a whole life waiting
In a chair beside you

If the years become a hallway
If the mirrors lose their grace
If the garden gets more quiet
And time touches your face

I will still know how to find you
Past the noise and past the flame
At the table by the river
Where the light still says your name

Let the piano run like water
Let the brass break through the night
Let the strings climb over Lisbon
Let the choir open light

Let the old stones hear us laughing
Let the river turn to gold
Let the room become a harbour
For the love we learned to hold

Lisbon keeps a chair for you
By the river, by the rain
Every light along the water
Leans to hear me say your name

Lisbon keeps a chair for you
In the blue behind the gold
Where the night becomes a singer
And the streets forget the cold

I do not need a kingdom
I do not need a view
There is a whole life waiting
In a chair beside you

There is a whole life waiting
In a chair beside you

Lisbon keeps a chair for you
And I am sitting next to you

tracks.title: Mercy Wears a Black Coat
tracks.lyrics:
Mercy came at midnight
Wearing rain upon her sleeve
She said, boy, I know your damage
But you still have to believe

I was sitting with my failures
In a room without a door
Drinking from the kind of silence
That don’t forgive no more

There were sirens in the distance
There were ghosts beneath the floor
There was love I never answered
Still knocking at my door

Mercy wears a black coat
Mercy walks through fire
Mercy does not flatter
Mercy does not lie

She came to cut me open
Not to make me clean
Mercy wears a black coat
And she came for me

I said I was only tired
I said I was only sad
I said everybody leaves things
They were never meant to have

But she looked at me like thunder
With a candle in her eyes
Said the worst kind of prison
Is the one you call your life

I had prayers without surrender
I had guilt without a name
I wanted all the healing
Without touching any flame

Mercy wears a black coat
Mercy walks through fire
Mercy does not flatter
Mercy does not lie

She came to cut me open
Not to make me clean
Mercy wears a black coat
And she came for me

And the choir said
Run no more
Run no more
Lay your weapons on the floor

And the drums said
Breathe again
Breathe again
You are not the worst you’ve been

Mercy wears a black coat
But her hands were made of light
She took the blade inside my chest
And turned it toward the night

I fell down like a sinner
I stood up like a man
Mercy wears a black coat
But I finally understand

She did not come to save me
She came to make me see
The wound was not my ending
It was only calling me

tracks.title: The Heart Comes Home At Night
tracks.filename: The Heart Comes Home At Night.mp3

tracks.title: Still We Sail
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
I left my name in ash
On a door that would not stay
Your coat still smells like rain
On the peg by the window frame

The maps all changed on me
The road got thin and cold
But I tied my hands to the wheel
And I watched the dark unfold

[Pre-Chorus]
Not brave
Just moved
By the shape of the bruise
If I stop here now
I lose more than I lose

[Chorus]
Still we sail, still we sail
Past the wreck and the hail
Still we sail, still we sail
What we fear feels less than stay

Still we sail, still we sail
With the ache and the trail
Still we sail, still we sail
Stopping now would cost us more

[Verse 2]
I kissed the edge of exile
With salt on my tongue
The harbor ghosted out
And the gulls kept circling up

I held your silence close
Like a stone in my coat
Some nights it pulled me under
Some nights it kept me afloat

[Pre-Chorus]
Not brave
Just moved
By the shape of the bruise
If I turn back now
I lose more than I lose

[Chorus]
Still we sail, still we sail
Past the wreck and the hail
Still we sail, still we sail
What we fear feels less than stay

Still we sail, still we sail
With the ache and the trail
Still we sail, still we sail
Stopping now would cost us more

[Bridge]
And if the sea takes names
Let it take the old ones too
I have learned what hunger means
I have learned what loss can do

So I lean into the black
Let the broken stars be proof
We are not made of courage
We are made of moving through

[Final Chorus]
Still we sail, still we sail
Past the wreck and the hail
Still we sail, still we sail
What we fear feels less than stay

Still we sail, still we sail
With the ache and the trail
Still we sail, still we sail
Stopping now would cost us more

tracks.title: Exile Body
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
I wake up here again
in this borrowed skin
My ribs know the route
my mouth learned to live with

A name in the pocket
worn thin at the fold
I touch every scar line
like map ink in gold

[Pre-Chorus]
Every border I crossed
is still under my hands
Every door I survived
left a mark where I stand

[Chorus]
My body, my country
my body, my country
I carry the lost home
like a bone in my sleeve

My body, my country
my body, my country
If exile comes looking
it finds me here, still breathing

[Verse 2]
The old rooms are vanished
the keys gone cold
But my heart keeps a census
of every street I sold

I sleep with the ruins
behind my eyes
And wake with the language
that keeps me alive

[Pre-Chorus]
Every border I crossed
is still under my hands
Every door I survived
left a mark where I stand

[Chorus]
My body, my country
my body, my country
I carry the lost home
like a bone in my sleeve

My body, my country
my body, my country
If exile comes looking
it finds me here, still breathing

[Bridge]
And if there’s no return
then let this be true
I was made from the leaving
but I stayed with you

No flag in the window
no soil I can claim
Just this scarred little kingdom
that answers my name

[Chorus]
My body, my country
my body, my country
I carry the lost home
like a bone in my sleeve

My body, my country
my body, my country
If exile comes looking
it finds me here, still breathing

tracks.title: The Accordion Breathes
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
You sat by the window frame
with your case on your knees
Old paint on your fingertips
and salt in your sleeve

You learned every room you left
by the shape of its door
Now the silence comes back
like you walking through it once more

[Pre-Chorus]
And I can hear you in the fold
in the creak, in the seam
Something old keeps wanting
to become the air in me

[Chorus]
The accordion breathes
The accordion breathes
It carries you home
through the dust and the reeds

The accordion breathes
The accordion breathes
Like a hand on my chest
like a name I still keep

[Verse 2]
It knows the train platform rain
the soap on the floor
The blue bowl by the sink
and your boots by the door

It remembers the feast
and the empty after
The way your laugh could bloom
then vanish in the rafters

[Pre-Chorus]
And I lean in close
when the bellows rise
Like the world is opening
with your eyes

[Chorus]
The accordion breathes
The accordion breathes
It carries you home
through the dust and the reeds

The accordion breathes
The accordion breathes
Like a hand on my chest
like a name I still keep

[Bridge]
Let it pull the air
from the rooms we knew
Let it bend that ache
into something true

If you are the gone
then stay in the song
In the push and the pull
where I still belong

[Final Chorus]
The accordion breathes
The accordion breathes
It carries you home
through the dust and the reeds

The accordion breathes
The accordion breathes
Like a hand on my chest
like a name I still keep

The accordion breathes
The accordion breathes
And I hear you now
in the space between me

tracks.title: Letters Never Crossed
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
I folded your name
In a blue lined sheet
Wrote "come back soon"
Then hid the creed

Left it in my drawer
By the tax receipts
Next to broken pens
And your extra key

[Pre-Chorus]
I had the words
Right on my tongue
Held them too long
Till the day was done

[Chorus]
Never mailed you
Never mailed you
Love with no address
Never mailed you
Never mailed you
Now it lives in my chest
(Sent it in my head)
(Too late, too late)

[Verse 2]
I wrote "I was scared"
At the bottom there
"I loved you hard"
But I never dared

Each stamp I bought
Stayed in the book
I kept your ghost
In a careful look

[Pre-Chorus]
I saw your porch
I saw your car
I turned around
With a pounding heart

[Chorus]
Never mailed you
Never mailed you
Love with no address
Never mailed you
Never mailed you
Now it lives in my chest
(Sent it in my head)
(Too late, too late)

[Bridge]
If you found the box
Would you know my hand?
Would you read my fear
And still understand?

I let the truth
Grow dust and rust
A love came whole
Then missed us

[Final Chorus]
Never mailed you
Never mailed you
Love with no address
Never mailed you
Never mailed you
Now it lives in my chest
(Sent it in my head)
(Too late, too late)

Never mailed you
Never mailed you
And I still can't forget
(Still can't forget)
(Still can't forget)

tracks.title: Salt Gospel
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
There is no church
but the open water
I came with my grief
like stones in my pocket

The waves took one name
then took the next one
I learned how to breathe
with my face in the salt

[Chorus]
Salt gospel, wash me clean
Salt gospel, carry me
What was lost goes under
What survives can sing
(Salt gospel)
(Salt gospel)

[Verse 2]
I buried my fear
in a coat of foam
Watched the dark turn pale
on the edge of dawn

The tide kept score
with a ruthless hand
Then it gave me back
what I could still stand

[Chorus]
Salt gospel, wash me clean
Salt gospel, carry me
What was lost goes under
What survives can sing
(Salt gospel)
(Salt gospel)

[Bridge]
If there is a prayer
it is hands in waves
If there is a heaven
it is this hard day

I was split open
I was set afloat
Now every scar
is a rope on my boat

[Final Chorus]
Salt gospel, wash me clean
Salt gospel, carry me
What was lost goes under
What survives can sing
Salt gospel, I am here
Salt gospel, I am here
(Salt gospel)
(Salt gospel)

tracks.title: She Who Stayed Behind
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
You packed the blue bowl
Left it by the sink
Said you'd write on Sundays
Said you'd need to think

I stood in the doorway
With your coat still near
Counted every footstep
Till the street went clear

[Pre-Chorus]
I swept your side of the table
Folded up your chair
I learned how a house can breathe
With only one soul there

[Chorus]
I stayed behind
I stayed behind
I kept the flame
I kept the line

Your name in the hall
Your shoes by the wall
I stayed behind
I stayed behind

[Verse 2]
Rain came through September
Tapped on every tin
I fixed the loose window
And let the dark come in

Neighbors asked me lightly
"How long has it been?"
I said "Just till the letters
Find their way back in"

[Pre-Chorus]
But seasons don't ask permission
They just turn and turn
Every plate I washed alone
Was a little lesson learned

[Chorus]
I stayed behind
I stayed behind
I kept the flame
I kept the line

Your name in the hall
Your shoes by the wall
I stayed behind
I stayed behind

[Bridge]
You get to be a story
Moving town to town
I am the porch and the hinge
The quiet settling down

I am the key in the dish
The jam on the shelf
I am the one who made a life
Out of waiting by myself

[Final Chorus]
I stayed behind
I stayed behind
I kept the flame
I kept the line

Your name in the hall
Your shoes by the wall
I stayed behind
I stayed behind

I stayed behind
I stayed behind
And when you came back wrong
I still knew how to hold this sound

tracks.title: Atlantic Nocturne
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The harbor slept in black silk
I met you where the water held its breath
No lantern on the rail
Just the long hush of the deck

Salt on our sleeves
And the night like a steady hand
It did not ask us anything
It only took our words and kept them

[Pre-Chorus]
And every wave said stay
Every shadow said stay
We were small, but we were safe
In the dark, the dark, the dark

[Chorus]
Atlantic nocturne
Hold me like this
Atlantic nocturne
Where the unsaid lives
The sea keeps our secrets
The sea keeps our names
Atlantic nocturne
Dream us again (dream us again)

[Verse 2]
I watched your face go silver
As a moon path split the water clean
You smiled like you knew
The things I could not mean

The old mast creaked
Like a door in a quiet house
And I let the night take over
What I could not bring out loud

[Pre-Chorus]
And every wave said stay
Every shadow said stay
We were small, but we were safe
In the dark, the dark, the dark

[Chorus]
Atlantic nocturne
Hold me like this
Atlantic nocturne
Where the unsaid lives
The sea keeps our secrets
The sea keeps our names
Atlantic nocturne
Dream us again (dream us again)

[Bridge]
If morning asks for answers
Let it find the shore alone
We can leave our doubts
In the riddle of the foam

Take the compass from my hands
Let the stars forget our trail
I would rather drift with you
Than wake and call it real

[Chorus]
Atlantic nocturne
Hold me like this
Atlantic nocturne
Where the unsaid lives
The sea keeps our secrets
The sea keeps our names
Atlantic nocturne
Dream us again (dream us again)

[Outro]
Darkness, my companion
Soft and wide and near
The ocean is a mirror
And I am clear in here

tracks.title: Grief in Different Latitudes
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Sunrise in Tokyo
Rain in Barcelona
A sigh in the desert wind
A tear on a frozen window pane
It’s the same old story
A heart in pieces again

[Prechorus]
Oh the weight it just hangs
A heavy cloud

[Chorus]
Grief animal
You roar you bite
Grief animal
You steal my light
Grief animal
You make me small
Grief animal
You take it all

[Verse 2]
A whispered prayer in the humid night
A shout across the endless plains
A quiet hum a burning ache
A silent scream a pouring rain
The language changes
The feeling stays the same

[Bridge]
From mountain high to ocean deep
This hollow echo where you sleep
A shadow dancing in my head
Words unspoken left unsaid
It’s a universal

tracks.title: The Port We Cannot Find
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
I packed my name in a paper bag
Boarded up dreams in a train car sack
Every town gave me half a sign
Then the rain washed out the line

I asked the dock men, I asked the shore
They pointed east, they pointed more
But every map I held so tight
Turned to smoke in the morning light

[Pre-Chorus]
Still I walk on
Still I go
With a bent old map
And a torn coat

[Chorus]
The port we cannot find
The port we cannot find
Maybe I was home
In the searching all the time

The port we cannot find
The port we cannot find
Every road I kept
Brought me here alive

[Verse 2]
I slept by fields with the fence line bent
Counted each loss like a week well spent
A woman laughed with a mouth full of corn
Said, "You been leaving since you were born"

I kept her words in my jacket seam
Like a matchbook cut from a better dream
And every mile that I thought was wrong
Turned my ache into a song

[Pre-Chorus]
Still I walk on
Still I go
With a bent old map
And a torn coat

[Chorus]
The port we cannot find
The port we cannot find
Maybe I was home
In the searching all the time

The port we cannot find
The port we cannot find
Every road I kept
Brought me here alive

[Bridge]
If I never see that line on the sea
If the gate stays shut for me
I will set my boots down slow
Where the river learns to go

And I’ll call it mine
I’ll call it mine
Not a place I lost
A place I chose in time

[Chorus]
The port we cannot find
The port we cannot find
Maybe I was home
In the searching all the time

The port we cannot find
The port we cannot find
Every road I kept
Brought me here alive

tracks.title: What Fado Holds
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Your cup still on the shelf
A ring mark in the glaze
The bread knife in the drawer
Still knows your careless ways
I fold the day back up
Like linen from the line
And every crease says clearly
You were here, and you were mine

[Pre-Chorus]
I touch the chair you left
It leans like it remembers
The quiet in the house
Is loud with old embers

[Chorus]
It is love
It is loss
It is both in one hand
It is the face in the dark
It is the name in the sand
I keep you
I let you go
That is all I can do
What fado holds
What fado holds
Is me still holding you

[Verse 2]
The neighbor’s radio
Carries a summer tune
I see us at the window
In the iron afternoon
You laughed with half a peach
And I with chipped blue plate
The world was small and shining
And I was not too late

[Pre-Chorus]
Now I know the blessing
That broke before I knew it
A blessed thing can vanish
And still you walk right through it

[Chorus]
It is love
It is loss
It is both in one hand
It is the face in the dark
It is the name in the sand
I keep you
I let you go
That is all I can do
What fado holds
What fado holds
Is me still holding you

[Bridge]
Let time be rough with me
Let the years take their due
I will not curse the waking
That once belonged to you
I will wear the tear
Like a medal on my sleeve
For nothing real is nothing
And I have learned to grieve

[Final Chorus]
It is love
It is loss
It is both in one hand
It is the face in the dark
It is the name in the sand
I keep you
I let you go
That is all I can do
What fado holds
What fado holds
Is me still holding you

tracks.title: Saudade Has a Weight
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Saudade sits down first
In the hollow of my chest
Like a stone in wet cloth
Like a coin I cannot spend

It leaves a print on my skin
Where your hand used to rest
A thin and living pressure
Under every quiet step

[Pre-Chorus]
I wash my wrists
It stays
I breathe in deep
It names me

[Chorus]
Saudade has a weight
Saudade has a weight
It bends my ribs at dusk
And makes the room feel late

Saudade has a weight
Saudade has a weight
A hand against my heart
That never slips away

[Verse 2]
In the mirror, my face
Looks softly overfilled
As if your missing shadow
Has taken up its fill

I feel it in the sheets
In the seam of folded clothes
In the pocket of my coat
Where your last small silence froze

[Pre-Chorus]
I turn away
It turns
I call your name
It learns

[Chorus]
Saudade has a weight
Saudade has a weight
It bends my ribs at dusk
And makes the room feel late

Saudade has a weight
Saudade has a weight
A hand against my heart
That never slips away

[Bridge]
If I could set it down
I would hear the table groan
I would mark the dent it made
And know what I have known

But it lives in tender places
Where the body keeps its proofs
In the pulse beneath the throat
In the skin between the roots

[Final Chorus]
Saudade has a weight
Saudade has a weight
It bends my ribs at dusk
And makes the room feel late

Saudade has a weight
Saudade has a weight
A hand against my heart
That never slips away

Saudade has a weight
Saudade has a weight
I carry what you left
And it carries me today

tracks.title: All the Water Between
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Your name fits in my hand
Like a coin I keep warm
Across the map, across the dark
You still hold your form

Midnight here, morning there
Still I feel the same pull
Like the tide keeps its promise
Even when the moon is full

[Pre-Chorus]
I don't need your shoulder
To know your weight
I don't need your footsteps
To know you stay

[Chorus]
All the water between
Can’t wash you out
All the water between
Still says your name out loud
You are here, you are real
In the shape you leave behind
All the water between
Still keeps us tied

[Verse 2]
There’s a cup by the sink
Still set for two
Some days that little detail
Is the best thing I can do

I read your old messages
Like prayer lines in a row
Not to bring you closer
Just to let the truth show

[Pre-Chorus]
I don't need the room to turn
For you to be
I don't need my eyes to lie
You live in me

[Chorus]
All the water between
Can’t wash you out
All the water between
Still says your name out loud
You are here, you are real
In the shape you leave behind
All the water between
Still keeps us tied

[Bridge]
If the world draws lines
I draw them back
With every memory
And every track

Time zone to time zone
We do not fade
Love is a real thing
Even when delayed

[Chorus]
All the water between
Can’t wash you out
All the water between
Still says your name out loud
You are here, you are real
In the shape you leave behind
All the water between
Still keeps us tied

[Outro]
Not missing
Not gone
Just far
And still mine

tracks.title: The Sea Keeps Our Names
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The dock was wet with rain
and salt on every sleeve
Marta held a paper bag
like it could help her breathe

My brother kissed the rail
then stared into the dark
We learned the names we carried
before we learned the sparks

[Chorus]
The sea keeps our names
the sea keeps our names
It takes what we bring
and still remembers

The sea keeps our names
the sea keeps our names
The brave, the lost, the changed
all the same to the waves

[Verse 2]
Some came with shoes in hand
some came with empty eyes
Some reached the other shore
and left their old lives behind

I saw a child wake up
with sand in her curled hair
She looked like two whole lives
were tangled in the air

[Chorus]
The sea keeps our names
the sea keeps our names
It takes what we bring
and still remembers

The sea keeps our names
the sea keeps our names
The brave, the lost, the changed
all the same to the waves

[Bridge]
For every face that vanished
for every voice that rose
For every soul that made it
scarred by what it knows

If you call out over water
it answers in its own way
Not with hands, not with mercy
but with what it can save

[Chorus]
The sea keeps our names
the sea keeps our names
It takes what we bring
and still remembers

The sea keeps our names
the sea keeps our names
The brave, the lost, the changed
all the same to the waves

tracks.title: I Chose the Open
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The room was black
The floor held still
I heard my name
Like a warning bell
I had the door
I had the key
But fear said stay
And look at me

[Pre-Chorus]
I tasted dust
I felt the bend
One more step
Or break at the end
Not for hope
Not for a sign
Just because
I was still alive

[Chorus]
I chose the open
I chose the open
When the dark got loud
I stayed in motion
I chose the open
I chose the open
Not for tomorrow
Just for devotion

[Verse 2]
The night bit hard
It took its share
Cold in my lungs
Sharp in the air
I saw what stops
What stays, what dies
Then I kept going
With shaking eyes

[Pre-Chorus]
I had the weight
I had the fear
I heard the truth
Too plain, too clear
If I go back
I lose my ground
So I moved on
Without a sound

[Chorus]
I chose the open
I chose the open
When the dark got loud
I stayed in motion
I chose the open
I chose the open
Not for tomorrow
Just for devotion

[Bridge]
Not because
The sky was kind
Not because
I knew I'd find
Some easy road
Some healed-up part
I moved because
It broke my heart

[Final Chorus]
I chose the open
I chose the open
When the dark got loud
I stayed in motion
I chose the open
I chose the open
Not for tomorrow
Just for devotion
I chose the open
I chose the open
And every hard step
Kept me whole again

tracks.title: Still We Sail (To Light)
tracks.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
We left with salt on sleeves,
and names we would not say.
The shore kept shrinking back
like it knew the price of stay.
I packed your photograph
inside my coat of rain.

[Pre-Chorus]
And all the miles
did not erase
the hands we lost,
the rooms we praised.

[Chorus]
Still we sail
through the ache
Still we sail
with a broken wake
Love stays
in the skin
Still we sail
to the light within

[Verse 2]
The border ate our hours,
the road kept asking why.
We learned to walk with ghosts
and keep the lantern dry.
Your laughter in the dark
was bread I could not buy.

[Pre-Chorus]
So let the wind
keep every scar
we are not whole,
but here we are.

[Chorus]
Still we sail
through the ache
Still we sail
with a broken wake
Love stays
in the skin
Still we sail
to the light within

[Bridge]
Not victory.
Not a crown.
Just two tired hearts
not putting down.
Saudade in the ribs,
salt in the blood,
we carry what was gone
and call it love.

[Chorus]
Still we sail
through the ache
Still we sail
with a broken wake
Love stays
in the skin
Still we sail
to the light within

tracks.title: Noiraciel - Angel of Darkness
tracks.album: The Blind Angel — Intimate Metal Sessions
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
Ooh
Ooh
Ooh

[Verse 1]
Beneath a black and silver sky
I watched the broken satellites
Fall like prayers that lost their way
Burning holes in heaven’s face

A crimson cloth across my eyes
But I could feel the world ignite
Steel and embers in the air
God was gone, but I was there

[Pre-Chorus]
Deep in the silence, I heard my name
Inside the ruin, inside the flame
No hand to save me, no light to trust
So I made a crown from the ash and dust

[Chorus]
I am the angel of darkness
Rising through fire, untouched by fear
I am the last sacred warning
The end of mercy is finally here
Out of the wreckage, I’m shining
Black-winged and burning, divine and heartless
Call me the fall of heaven
Call me the angel of darkness

[Post-Chorus]
Oh-oh
Angel of darkness
Oh-oh
I rise
Oh-oh
Angel of darkness
Out of the fire tonight

[Verse 2]
The city trembled red and gold
A dying empire turning cold
Sirens crying through the glass
Like ghosts afraid to make it last

My wings were scarred, but standing wide
A storm of metal, flame and light
Every wound became a star
Every scar revealed what we are

[Pre-Chorus]
Deep in the silence, I heard my name
Inside the ruin, inside the flame
No hand to save me, no light to trust
So I made a throne from the ash and dust

[Chorus]
I am the angel of darkness
Rising through fire, untouched by fear
I am the last sacred warning
The end of mercy is finally here
Out of the wreckage, I’m shining
Black-winged and burning, divine and heartless
Call me the fall of heaven
Call me the angel of darkness

[Bridge]
Piano like thunder
Strings like a tide
Brass in my bloodstream
Night on my side

I don’t need halos
I don’t need grace
I looked into chaos
And gave it a face

I turned the silence
Into command
I pulled the future
Out of my hands

[Build]
Higher
Higher through the flame
Higher
Nothing stays the same
Higher
Let the whole world watch me rise
With heaven burning in my eyes

[Final Chorus]
I am the angel of darkness
Born from the wound where the lost ones start
I am the voice in the skyline
The final beat of a dying heart
Out of the wreckage, I’m shining
No longer holy, but more than flawless
Call me the end of the old world
Call me the angel of darkness

[Outro]
Oh-oh
Angel of darkness
Oh-oh
I rise
Oh-oh
Angel of darkness
I am the fire in the night

tracks.title: Noiraciel - Ashes of Heaven
tracks.album: The Blind Angel — Intimate Metal Sessions
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
Ooh
Ooh
Ooh
Ashes of heaven

[Verse 1]
The sky came down in shards of gold
A dying myth, a lie once told
The gates were burning, slow and pale
Like holy light too weak to stay

I felt the dust upon my skin
Like all of time collapsing in
The stars were falling one by one
And silence swallowed every sun

[Pre-Chorus]
No choirs calling
No voices left
Only the ruin
Inside my chest
If this is ending
Then let it begin
With ashes of heaven
Falling again

[Chorus]
We are the ashes of heaven
Scattered in fire, carried by night
Born from the end of devotion
Still reaching up for the light
Out of the wreckage we rise now
Out of the silence we learn
If paradise died above us
Then let the whole sky burn

[Post-Chorus]
Ashes of heaven
Ashes of heaven
Falling through me tonight

[Verse 2]
The towers echoed with the sound
Of broken halos hitting ground
A thousand prayers without a home
Drifting like smoke through flesh and bone

My wings were heavy, torn and wide
Still full of thunder deep inside
I walked through ruins dressed in flame
And nothing in me looked away

[Pre-Chorus]
No god to answer
No hand to guide
Only the embers
I kept alive
If faith is over
Then let it end
With ashes of heaven
Falling again

[Chorus]
We are the ashes of heaven
Scattered in fire, carried by night
Born from the end of devotion
Still reaching up for the light
Out of the wreckage we rise now
Out of the silence we learn
If paradise died above us
Then let the whole sky burn

[Bridge]
Piano in the distance
Cold and clear
Like memory refusing
To disappear

Strings like oceans
Pulling me under
Brass like judgment
Far away thunder

I held the dust
Like it could speak
I held the dark
Like it was me
And in the last glow
Before the fall
I heard the dead stars
Call my name through it all

[Build]
Higher
Through the smoke and fire
Higher
Through the funeral choir
Higher
Even if the angels drown
I will not go down

[Final Chorus]
We are the ashes of heaven
Scattered in fire, carried by night
Born from the end of devotion
Still becoming more than light
Out of the wreckage we rise now
No longer praying to return
If paradise died above us
Then let the whole sky burn

[Outro]
Ashes of heaven
On my tongue
Ashes of heaven
In my lungs
Ashes of heaven
Still glowing in the dark

tracks.title: Noiraciel - Blood on the Halo
tracks.album: The Blind Angel — Intimate Metal Sessions
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro – Clean, low / ritual]
Gold around my head
Red across my hands
What was once called holy
No longer understands

[Verse 1 – Clean]
The organ hummed through velvet smoke
As marble saints began to choke
A silver light turned bruised and red
Like heaven blushing for the dead

The violins moved sharp and slow
Like silk dragged lightly over bone
Low horns rolled under every breath
A kiss, a curse, a hint of death

[Pre-Chorus – Clean building]
I wore devotion
Like perfume and chains
I touched the sacred
And left it stained

[Pre-Chorus – Growl layer]
No pure confession
No sin to conceal
Only the mark
Of what is real

[Chorus – Clean + Growl Layer]
Blood on the halo
Red in the light where innocence died
Blood on the halo
Love and destruction braided inside
Raise the choir
Lower the blade
Let the beautiful learn what the beautiful made
Blood on the halo
I wore the wound
And called it divine

[Post-Chorus – Growl accent]
[Growl]
Blood
On the halo

[Verse 2 – Clean]
The piano fell in blackened pearls
Across a throne of ash and girls
The bells were soft, the drums were deep
Like something holy gone to sleep

My wings were wide with burnished scars
Still catching fire from dying stars
And every gaze that met my face
Could feel the poison under grace

[Pre-Chorus – Clean]
I gave them beauty
I gave them fear
I made the distant
Feel too near

[Pre-Chorus – Growl layer]
No saint above me
No clean design
I touched the light
And made it mine

[Chorus – Clean + Growl Layer]
Blood on the halo
Red in the light where innocence died
Blood on the halo
Love and destruction braided inside
Raise the choir
Lower the blade
Let the beautiful learn what the beautiful made
Blood on the halo
I wore the wound
And called it divine

[Bridge – Clean / Choir / Growl]
[Clean]
Grand piano trembling under lace and flame
Strings circling slowly around my name
French horns blooming like a royal sin
And I leaned in
I leaned in

[Choir]
Sancta no more
Sancta no more

[Growl]
Break the mirror
Crack the gold
Show them what the myths don’t hold
Skin and marble
Breath and wire
I made holiness
Taste desire

[Breakdown – Growl]
Drag the silk through burning oil
Make the altar learn recoil
Split the pearl and flood the white
Turn the blessing into bite
No forgiveness
No disguise
Only hunger dressed as light

[Orchestral Lift – Clean]
Hear the cellos pull me lower
Hear the violins draw blood
Hear the brass kiss through the ruin
Like a crown inside a flood

[Final Chorus – Clean + Huge Growl Layer + Choir]
Blood on the halo
Red in the light where innocence died
Blood on the halo
Love and destruction braided inside
Raise the choir
Lower the blade
Let the beautiful learn what the beautiful made
Blood on the halo
I wore the wound
And called it divine

[Final Lift – Clean / Choir]
Blood on the halo
No untouched heaven left to find
Blood on the halo
Every scar now burns like wine
Blood on the halo
Every prayer now tastes like fire
Blood on the halo
I took the fall
And dressed it in desire

[Outro – Clean low]
Gold around my head
Red across my hands
What was once called holy
No longer understands

tracks.title: Noiraciel - Broken Wings, Burning Soul
tracks.album: The Blind Angel — Intimate Metal Sessions
tracks.lyrics:
15. Broken Wings, Burning Soul

[Intro – Clean, intimate / cinematic]
My wings were never made
To leave this world untouched
They broke against the stars
But they still carried fire

[Verse 1 – Clean]
The piano fell through frozen air
Like memory learning how to tear
The skyline glowed in wounded gold
A final warmth against the cold

The violins moved pale and wide
Like ghosts that would not choose a side
Low cellos pulled beneath my skin
Where all the oldest wars begin

[Pre-Chorus – Clean building]
I gave the sky
More than I had
I gave the light
The best of me

[Pre-Chorus – Growl layer entering softly]
And when it left
And left me torn
The fire stayed
Inside my grief

[Chorus – Clean + Growl Layer]
Broken wings, burning soul
I fell apart but I did not let go
Broken wings, burning soul
Half made of ruin, half made of gold
Raise the strings
Raise the flame
Let the dark remember my name
Broken wings, burning soul
Still rising
Still whole

[Post-Chorus – Low Clean]
Still rising
Still whole

[Verse 2 – Clean]
The organ breathed below the storm
Like something ancient keeping warm
Brass rolled slow behind the haze
Like dying suns refusing graves

My feathers carried ash and light
Black at the edges, gold inside
And every scar across my back
Was proof that I had made it back

[Pre-Chorus – Clean]
I touched the ground
It held my weight
I touched the pain
It opened space

[Pre-Chorus – Growl layer]
No saint to save me
No hand to hold
Only the furnace
Inside my soul

[Chorus – Clean + Growl Layer]
Broken wings, burning soul
I fell apart but I did not let go
Broken wings, burning soul
Half made of ruin, half made of gold
Raise the strings
Raise the flame
Let the dark remember my name
Broken wings, burning soul
Still rising
Still whole

[Bridge – Clean / Choir / Growl]
[Clean]
Grand piano under a blood-grey sky
Strings lifting slowly as the years went by
French horns calling from the other side
Like all I lost still walked beside me

[Choir]
Rise though broken
Rise though broken

[Growl]
Tear the feather
Break the bone
I kept the fire
I kept the soul
Burn the weakness
Strip the role
You can wound the wing
Not the whole

[Breakdown – Growl]
I was never made for easy flight
Never made for untouched white
I was made for impact, flame, and strain
For sacred loss and holy pain
Let the bells ring through the scar
Let the wound become a star
There is no true becoming whole
Without the burn inside the soul

[Orchestral Lift – Clean]
Hear the cellos pull me upward
Hear the choir open the sky
Hear the brass turn grief to power
Hear the broken learn to fly

[Final Chorus – Clean + Huge Growl Layer + Choir]
Broken wings, burning soul
I fell apart but I did not let go
Broken wings, burning soul
Half made of ruin, half made of gold
Raise the strings
Raise the flame
Let the dark remember my name
Broken wings, burning soul
Still rising
Still whole

[Final Lift – Clean / Choir]
Broken wings, burning soul
No clean ending, no perfect role
Broken wings, burning soul
Still enough to shake the throne
Broken wings, burning soul
Every scar became control
Broken wings, burning soul
I kept the fire
I kept the soul

[Outro – Clean low]
My wings were never made
To leave this world untouched
They broke against the stars
But they still carried fire

tracks.title: Noiraciel - Crown of Fire
tracks.album: The Blind Angel — Intimate Metal Sessions
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro – Choir / Clean low]
Gold in the ashes
Flame in the wire
I did not inherit
I built this crown of fire

[Verse 1 – Clean]
The organ shook the hollow sky
As towers bent and sirens died
Red embers fell like shattered stars
Across the ruins and the scars

Low cellos dragged the earth below
While taikos rolled like distant war
The brass came down in burning waves
And opened up forgotten graves

[Pre-Chorus – Clean building]
No hand to crown me
No throne to claim
No god to bless me
No holy name

[Pre-Chorus – Growl layer]
I forged the metal
I fed the pyre
I took the dark
And made it higher

[Chorus – Clean + Growl Layer]
Crown of fire
Set on my head by the end of the world
Crown of fire
Forged from the ruin where heaven unfurled
Raise the horns
Raise the drums
Let the last procession come
Crown of fire
I wear the flame
And make the apocalypse kneel

[Post-Chorus – Growl]
[Growl]
Crown
Of fire

[Verse 2 – Clean]
The violins cut through the smoke
Like silver vows already broken
Black banners danced in molten air
And every wound was standing there

My wings were iron, wide and scarred
Still catching sparks from falling stars
No mercy left, no peace to borrow

[Pre-Chorus – Clean]
I kissed the furnace
I took the brand
I stood unshaken

[Pre-Chorus – Growl layer]

With the end of time

[Chorus – Clean + Growl Layer]
Crown of fire
Set on my head by the end of the world
Crown of fire
Forged from the ruin where heaven unfurled
Raise the horns
Raise the drums
Let the last procession come
Crown of fire
I wear the flame
And make the apocalypse kneel

[Bridge – Clean / Choir / Growl]
[Clean]
Grand piano falling like cathedral rain
Strings climbing higher through the pain
French horns calling from the other side
And still I rise
And still I rise

[Choir]
Ignis rex
Ignis rex

[Growl]
Bass trombones tearing
Stone from bone
War drums calling
Take the throne
Tubular bells in the blood-red spire
I was not chosen
